Then give each other time to ask open questions before discussing your progress toward goals. }} == \uD83E\uDD45 Goals and progress == <!-- Fill in the table before your meeting. Add questions about what you learned since the last meeting and what you hope to achieve by the next meeting. --> {| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="ec8025fc8bb4" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="405b4e4d5537" ! ac:local-id="50e7b7a1a5f7" data-highlight-colour="#ffebe6"| '''Participants''' ! ac:local-id="35d1cc5daf9b" data-highlight-colour="#ffebe6"| '''Priorities since we last met''' ! ac:local-id="51d990aa66f8" data-highlight-colour="#ffebe6"| '''Priorities until we meet again''' |- ac:local-id="e5e7733b0310" ! ac:local-id="960f5d25abb2" data-highlight-colour="#ffffff"| <!-- @ mention the first participant --> | ac:local-id="e5a83b76fab5"| * <!-- e.g., What did you learn about the project since we last met? --> *
| ac:local-id="0eaaad40e595"| * <!-- e.g., How should we prepare for next week's sales presentation? --> *
|- ac:local-id="2b38d0642bc7" ! ac:local-id="eb06930dc8cf" data-highlight-colour="#ffffff"| <!-- @ mention the second participant --> | ac:local-id="7a9cda6e5ff3"| * <!-- e.g., Has anything slowed down or blocked work recently? --> *
| ac:local-id="e697e005affb"| * <!-- e.g., How much progress have we made on our quarterly goals? --> * |} == \uD83C\uDF1F Important topics == <!-- Add discussion topics before the meeting. --> {| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d914ecd27c54" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="b3ea2ac901ed" ! class="numberingColumn"| ! ac:local-id="79730bcca919"| '''Topic''' ! ac:local-id="9dc60a45cc30"| '''Description''' ! ac:local-id="7554c65a4d11"| '''Results''' |- ac:local-id="83c35902354a" | class="numberingColumn"| 1 | ac:local-id="08ad2f7123ee"| <!-- State the topic --> | ac:local-id="986e7b8c7afb"| <!-- Describe the topic and explain how it impacts work --> | ac:local-id="864d5866bbe1"| <!-- Note the results and decisions made during the meeting --> |- ac:local-id="3077ee99337a" | class="numberingColumn"| 2 | ac:local-id="79a92f3cf488"| | ac:local-id="79636c9c6905"| | ac:local-id="56353192903e"| |- ac:local-id="caacb32e656f" | class="numberingColumn"| 3 | ac:local-id="f6989aee5464"| | ac:local-id="c796f4dcd192"| | ac:local-id="f12a068f49f0"| |} == [[Category:Broken_emoticon]] Action items == <!-- Add action items that you create during the meeting and check off boxes as you make progress. -->

This documentation provides a high-level overview of standard Helpdesk procedures, outlining how support requests are received, handled, escalated, and resolved to ensure consistent and efficient service delivery.
[[Category:Broken_macro/excerpt]]
This is another test excerpt.
[[Category:Broken_macro/excerpt]]{{SubpageList|all=true|depth=0|allChildren=true|style=|sortAndReverse=|first=0|page=}} __NOTOC__

__TOC__ == '''Overview''' == This document describes the standard '''Ticket Workflow''' used by the IT department.
The workflow ensures that all support issues are processed consistently, tracked efficiently, and resolved within agreed timelines. All employees should follow this process when reporting, handling, or escalating support tickets. == Workflow diagram == {{Drawio|mVer=2 |simple=0 |zoom=1 |inComment=0 |pageId=867926019 |custContentId=869629965 |diagramDisplayName=Untitled Diagram-1773843111297.drawio |lbox=1 |contentVer=1 |revision=1 |baseUrl=https://geht.atlassian.net/wiki |diagramName=Migration_Ticket_Workflow-Untitled_Diagram-1773843111297.drawio |pCenter=0 |width=540 |links= |tbstyle= |height=430 }} ----- == '''Ticket creation''' == A ticket is created when a user reports an issue through one of the approved channels: * '''IT Support Portal''' (preferred method) * '''Email to support@company.local''' * '''Automated system alerts''' (monitoring tools) Each new ticket must include: * Clear problem description * Affected system(s) * Urgency / business impact * Steps already taken by the user (if any) * Relevant attachments (logs, screenshots) '''Ownership:''' Upon creation, the ticket is automatically assigned to the '''IT Service Desk''' queue. ----- == '''Initial triage''' == The Service Desk performs triage within '''30 minutes''' (during business hours).
Triage steps include: # '''Review the issue''' and confirm necessary details. # '''Categorize''' (e.g., Software, Hardware, Network, Access). # '''Prioritize''': #* P1 – critical (system down, major impact) #* P2 – high (significant impairment, no workaround) #* P3 – medium (non-urgent functional issue) #* P4 – low (general questions, cosmetic issues)
[[Migration:Team_Site_IT/IT_Support_&_Operations/Helpdesk_Procedures/Priority_Definitions|-> see priority definitions.]] # '''Assign''' the ticket to the appropriate resolver group. If information is missing, the Service Desk sets the ticket to '''“Pending User”''' and requests the needed details. ----- == '''Investigation and resolution''' == Once assigned, the resolver group is responsible for: * Reproducing or confirming the issue * Investigating logs, configurations, and dependencies * Applying a fix, workaround, or recommended next step * Documenting all actions taken in the ticket * Coordinating with other teams if needed If progress is blocked due to external dependency (vendor support, user response, awaiting hardware), the ticket status should be set to '''“Pending External”''' or '''“Pending User.”''' → [[Migration:Team_Site_IT/IT_Support_&_Operations/Helpdesk_Procedures/SLAs|see SLAs.]] ----- == '''Escalation''' == Escalation occurs when a ticket cannot be resolved within its SLA or requires higher authority. '''Types of escalation:''' * '''Functional Escalation''' – Handing off to a more specialized team. * '''Hierarchical Escalation''' – Notifying IT leadership for urgent or SLA‑breaching issues. Escalation guidelines: * Escalate '''P1''' issues immediately if not solvable within 1 hour. * Escalate '''P2–P4''' if no progress is made after one full business day. All escalation steps must be documented in the ticket. ----- == '''Ticket closure''' == A ticket may be closed only when: # The issue is resolved or an accepted workaround is in place. # Documentation of the fix is complete. # The user confirms resolution '''or''' no response is received after two follow‑ups within 48 hours. Closure steps: * Set status to '''“Resolved”''' and include a summary of actions. * Change to '''“Closed”''' after confirmation or timeout. * If applicable, add a knowledge entry for repeatable solutions. ----- == '''Quality and continuous improvement''' == IT periodically reviews ticket statistics to: * Identify recurring problems * Improve documentation * Adjust monitoring and automation * Refine internal workflows Monthly reporting includes metrics for volume, SLA compliance, category trends, and resolution times. {{AttachmentsSectionStart}} * [[Media:Migration_Ticket_Workflow-Untitled_Diagram-1773843111297.drawio]] * [[Media:Migration_Ticket_Workflow-Untitled_Diagram-1773843111297.drawio.png]] {{AttachmentsSectionEnd}}

== IT Support & Operations == {{SubpageList|all=true|depth=0|allChildren=true|style=|page=Migration:Team Site IT/IT Support & Operations|sortAndReverse=|first=0}} == Infrastructure & Systems == {{SubpageList|all=true|depth=0|allChildren=true|style=|page=Migration:Team Site IT/Infrastructure & Systems|sortAndReverse=|first=0}} __NOTOC__

__TOC__ == '''Overview''' == This document provides an overview of the company’s network map, including the logical layout of network segments, core infrastructure components, and key interconnections. The network map serves as a reference for IT operations, troubleshooting, system planning, and security assessments. The network map is maintained by the IT department and updated whenever new infrastructure is added, removed, or modified. ----- == '''Purpose of the network map''' == The network map is used to: * Visualize how systems and network components are connected * Understand traffic flow between VLANs, servers, and external services * Assist in troubleshooting connectivity issues * Support security audits and firewall rule creation * Provide clarity during onboarding of IT staff and during project planning ----- == '''Network layers represented''' == The network map includes several logical layers to reflect the full environment: === '''Core layer''' === The core layer includes: * Main switches * Routing infrastructure * Redundant uplinks * Firewalls * Internet and WAN gateways This layer represents high‑speed, fault‑tolerant connections that form the backbone of the network. === '''Distribution layer''' === The distribution layer connects core networking components to: * Access switches * Server clusters * Virtualization hosts * Aggregation switches for building floors or departments This layer manages routing between major VLANs and security zones. === '''Access layer''' === The access layer includes: * Office workstation switches * Wireless access points * IoT device networks * Printer and small‑office equipment This is where end‑user devices connect to the network. ----- == '''Key components displayed''' == The network map typically shows the following: === '''Switching infrastructure''' === * Core switches (Layer‑3) * Distribution and access switches * Redundant switch links * Port channel or link aggregation paths === '''Firewall and security zones''' === * Primary and secondary firewalls * DMZ segments * Internal security zones (e.g., user, server, management, IoT VLANs) === '''Server and virtualization systems''' === * Application servers * Database servers * Hypervisors * Storage networks (SAN/NAS) === '''Wireless infrastructure''' === * Wireless controllers * Access point groups by building or floor * Guest Wi‑Fi segmentation === '''WAN and internet connections''' === * ISP handoff points * VPN concentrators * Remote site tunnels (if applicable) ----- == '''Update and maintenance''' == The network map must remain accurate and up‑to‑date. The IT department is responsible for: * Updating the map after infrastructure changes * Reviewing and validating the map quarterly * Version‑controlling diagrams * Archiving outdated versions for reference All updates must be documented and approved according to the change management process. ----- == '''Storage location''' == The official network map is stored in: * The internal documentation system * The centralized network share (read‑only) * The IT operations folder within the knowledge management tool Only the IT department has write access.

== '''Overview''' == This document defines the priority levels used by the IT department when handling support tickets, incidents, and service requests. Clear priority definitions ensure consistent handling, realistic expectations, and compliance with internal SLAs. ----- == '''Priority 1 (P1) – critical''' == '''Description:'''
A major outage or severe issue causing immediate and significant business impact. '''Typical scenarios:''' * Production system is down * Company‑wide connectivity issues * Security breach or critical vulnerability * No workaround available '''Impact:'''
Business operations are halted or at severe risk. '''SLA targets:''' * '''Response:''' 15 minutes * '''Resolution:''' 4 hours ----- == '''Priority 2 (P2) – high''' == '''Description:'''
A high‑impact issue affecting multiple users, teams, or key functionality. '''Typical scenarios:''' * Department‑wide tool malfunction * Significant performance issues * Important service degraded with no workable workaround '''Impact:'''
Business operations remain functional but are significantly hindered. '''SLA targets:''' * '''Response:''' 1 hour * '''Resolution:''' 1 business day ----- == '''Priority 3 (P3) – medium''' == '''Description:'''
Standard user issues or service requests that affect productivity but have a workaround available. '''Typical scenarios:''' * Non‑critical software bug * Access requests blocking a specific task * Localized workstation issues '''Impact:'''
Limited, localized impact on business operations. '''SLA targets:''' * '''Response:''' 4 hours * '''Resolution:''' 3 business days ----- == '''Priority 4 (P4) – low''' == '''Description:'''
Non‑urgent requests, cosmetic issues, or general questions. '''Typical scenarios:''' * UI improvements * Minor display issues * How‑to questions * Scheduled maintenance '''Impact:'''
Minimal or no business disruption. '''SLA targets:''' * '''Response:''' 1 business day * '''Resolution:''' 5 business days ----- == '''How priorities are determined''' == When assigning a priority, IT evaluates: * '''Business impact''' – Who or what is affected? * '''Urgency''' – How quickly is intervention required? * '''Workarounds''' – Can users continue working? * '''Scope''' – One user, one team, or company‑wide? Priorities must be adjusted if impact or urgency changes. ----- == '''Reclassification of priorities''' == Priorities may be upgraded or downgraded if: * New information becomes available * Impact increases or decreases * A workaround is discovered or removed * SLA risk is detected All priority changes must be documented in the ticket, including justification. __NOTOC__

__TOC__ == '''Introduction''' == Welcome to the official '''TaskRules User Manual'''.
This document serves as a full-length reference guide for administrators, process owners, and end‑users working with TaskRules—our workflow and checklist creation system deeply integrated with '''InsightHub''' documentation. TaskRules enables organizations to turn knowledge into action by connecting written documentation with executable, step‑by‑step workflows. It is ideal for: * Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) execution * Employee onboarding * Recurring operational processes * Compliance-driven checklists * Quality assurance routines * Team knowledge handovers The purpose of this manual is to provide a single, comprehensive page that explains how to use TaskRules from installation to expert-level functionality. ----- == '''What is TaskRules?''' == TaskRules is a knowledge‑driven process execution platform. At its core:
'''TaskRules creates workflows and checklists tied to InsightHub documentation.'''
'''Ideal for SOP execution, onboarding, or standard processes.'''
Unlike traditional digital checklist tools, TaskRules links every workflow step to its originating InsightHub article. This guarantees that: * Workflows stay synchronized with official documentation * Users always have context behind each task * Updates to documentation automatically cascade into TaskRules * Processes remain consistent across teams ----- == '''Key features''' == === '''Workflow Builder''' === A visual, drag‑and‑drop designer used to create structured workflows.
Features include: * Step groups * Conditional branching * Parallel task lanes * Approval gates * Deadlines and reminders * Embedded InsightHub references === '''Checklist execution mode''' === Each workflow transforms into an actionable checklist that users can execute: * Mark steps as complete * Add comments, attachments, or screenshots * Pause and resume progress * Share visibility with team members * Automatically generate run histories === '''InsightHub integration''' === TaskRules integrates natively with InsightHub: * Link workflow steps to articles or sections * Display embedded documentation in the workflow sidebar * Track version changes of referenced documents === '''Templates library''' === Reusable templates allow teams to build standardized processes quickly: * IT onboarding * HR hiring workflow * Software deployment pipeline * Monthly maintenance checklists * Quality management tasks === '''Reporting and analytics''' === TaskRules provides transparency into operational process execution: * Workflow run analytics * User productivity views * Completion rates * Reopened task insights * Compliance tracking ----- == '''Getting started''' == === '''Accessing TaskRules''' === TaskRules is available through: * The web application (recommended) * Embedded InsightHub navigation * SSO-enabled login (if configured) === '''User roles''' === {| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="5796306b-e31f-4314-be3d-74569c85a714" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="0f9ea4ed-eaa9-4b3a-ba91-a04d5aa4f27f" ! ac:local-id="f6765747-46b7-4f8a-8b24-3038e42ee802"| Role ! ac:local-id="5812434f-79d0-474a-982f-640d3d0df126"| Permissions |- ac:local-id="c6148087-d2b1-4aa6-9d5c-ba64ad9c1d83" ! ac:local-id="fb9ef140-4dcf-4ea7-898e-f77f1c457568"| '''Viewer''' | ac:local-id="de1ae0ed-8fd0-4a8a-887c-8883f0329056"| View and execute workflows/checklists |- ac:local-id="7d9dd091-8c6e-4578-be30-baaa60eb1ff6" ! ac:local-id="f90649ba-9153-4157-85bc-25d4df9700d4"| '''Contributor''' | ac:local-id="653daa8e-2030-4022-881e-5eb2e3b5ec0b"| Create workflows, edit steps, run checklists |- ac:local-id="7e825d9c-d699-42c5-a959-bf596bef0bf2" ! ac:local-id="23abf5da-2a12-4e91-b580-f2e32f3faedf"| '''Owner''' | ac:local-id="0649f8a4-f02c-4ca4-82ab-24902f6f9669"| Manage workflow permissions, versions, and templates |- ac:local-id="245a0e56-3b8d-4aaa-ba9f-1983b203087b" ! ac:local-id="cd75ce73-0d01-4e6b-8e6d-5616a3e8d84c"| '''Administrator''' | ac:local-id="c25747c1-d59b-4447-a3c8-49022ac986e6"| System-wide settings, integrations, permission audits |} === '''Navigating the dashboard''' === The dashboard displays: * Recent workflows * Assigned checklists * Draft workflows * Pending approvals * Analytics shortcuts A global search bar helps users find workflows, templates, and documentation instantly. ----- == '''Creating workflows''' == === '''Starting a new workflow''' === To create a new workflow: # Navigate to '''Workflows → Create New''' # Enter a name, description, and category # Choose a base template (optional) # Start building with the Workflow Builder === '''Adding steps''' === TaskRules supports various step types: * '''Standard step''' – A simple action to perform * '''Documentation step''' – Linked directly to InsightHub * '''Approval step''' – Requires a designated approver * '''Conditional step''' – Logic-based branching * '''Parallel step''' – Tasks executed simultaneously * '''Form step''' – Users fill out fields during workflow execution === '''Linking InsightHub documentation''' === To link a step with documentation: # Click '''Attach documentation''' # Search InsightHub # Select an article or heading # Confirm and preview the embedded context Linked articles appear in a collapsible pane alongside the step. === '''Organizing steps into groups''' === Groups help structure complex workflows: * Preparation * Execution * Quality Assurance * Wrap-up Each group can include deadlines, responsible teams, and custom metadata. ----- == '''Running checklists''' == === '''Starting a checklist run''' === Users can start a run by: * Clicking '''Run workflow''' * Using a scheduled trigger * Starting via automation (if enabled) === '''Completing steps''' === During execution, users may: * Mark steps as done * Attach files * Record notes * Request assistance * Flag issues Each action is timestamped for auditability. === '''Pausing and resuming''' === Checklists can be: * Paused * Restarted * Continued from any completed step Ideal for multi-day or multi-phase processes. === '''Run history''' === Every checklist generates: * Completion timeline * Responsible users * Step-by-step logs * Linked documentation versions * Deviations and flagged issues ----- == '''Advanced features''' == === '''Conditional logic''' === Rules allow workflows to adapt dynamically.
Examples: * “If the employee is remote, include the remote onboarding steps.” * “If the system check fails, start a remediation path.” === '''Role-based steps''' === Allow task assignment to: * Specific users * Teams * Dynamic groups (e.g., “current checklist runner”) === '''Automation triggers''' === Workflows can be automated to start: * On a recurring schedule * When an InsightHub document is updated * When a specific form is submitted * Based on external API triggers === '''Integrations''' === TaskRules supports: * InsightHub (native) * Slack or Teams notifications * Email digests * Webhooks * Upcoming: Jira & ServiceNow connectors ----- == '''Best practices''' == === '''Keep steps action-oriented''' === Good step example: * “Verify that the deployment log shows no errors.” Bad example: * “Deployment logs.” === '''Link documentation early''' === Always attach relevant InsightHub content while building the workflow. === '''Use groups for clarity''' === Break workflows into logical parts. === '''Avoid over-complexity''' === Use conditional steps only when necessary. ----- == '''Troubleshooting''' == === '''Workflow not loading''' === Possible causes: * Missing permissions * Network connection issues * Workflow version archived === '''InsightHub links broken''' === Check whether: * The original document was deleted * The article was moved * You have access permissions === '''Checklist cannot start''' === Verify: * Workflow is published * You have permission to run the workflow * Required integrations are active ----- == '''FAQ''' == '''Q: Can I run multiple checklists at once?'''
Yes, TaskRules supports unlimited parallel runs. '''Q: Can workflows be exported?'''
Exports are supported to JSON format. '''Q: Does TaskRules support mobile devices?'''
Yes, TaskRules is fully responsive on modern browsers. ----- == '''Glossary''' == * '''Workflow''' – A structured sequence of steps. * '''Checklist''' – An executable version of a workflow. * '''Run History''' – The audit trail for a completed checklist. * '''InsightHub''' – The documentation system integrated with TaskRules. * '''SOP''' – Standard Operating Procedure. ----- == '''Appendix A: Example Workflows''' == === '''New employee onboarding (Sample)''' === # Prepare workspace # Set up accounts # Provide equipment # Conduct orientation # Assign initial tasks === '''Monthly maintenance checklist''' === * Patch operating systems * Update dependencies * Run security scans * Document changes ----- == '''Appendix B: Template library overview''' == Includes predefined templates for: * HR onboarding * IT support procedures * Factory equipment inspection * Software release process * Customer support escalation ----- == '''Conclusion''' == TaskRules is built to help teams turn knowledge into clear, repeatable action.
By tying workflows directly to InsightHub documentation, TaskRules ensures consistency, quality, and accountability across all organizational processes. For additional support, contact the TaskRules documentation team or visit the internal support portal.

__TOC__ == '''Overview''' == This document outlines the company’s VLAN (Virtual Local Area Network) structure, its purpose, and the general guidelines for defining and maintaining VLANs within the corporate network. VLAN segmentation improves security, performance, and manageability by logically separating traffic across shared network hardware. ----- == '''VLAN design principles''' == The company’s VLAN structure follows these design principles: * '''Segmentation by function''' – Devices are grouped by purpose (e.g., workstations, servers, voice). * '''Separation of security zones''' – Sensitive systems (e.g., finance, HR, production systems) operate within isolated VLANs. * '''Consistent numbering''' – VLAN IDs follow a standardized numbering scheme for easier identification. * '''Minimized broadcast domains''' – VLANs are sized to avoid excessive broadcast traffic and minimize latency. * '''Controlled routing''' – Traffic between VLANs is permitted only through defined Layer‑3 rules and firewall policies. ----- == '''VLAN categories''' == The company utilizes the following common VLAN categories: === '''User VLANs''' === User VLANs support employee workstations, laptops, and office endpoints. These are organized by: * Department * Physical location (office floor, building zone) * Security requirements User VLANs generally have internet access but restricted access to internal systems. === '''Server VLANs''' === Server VLANs contain on‑premises servers, virtual machines, and application backends. These VLANs: * Have stricter firewall rules * Allow intra‑server communication only where permitted * Are segmented based on system sensitivity and purpose Examples include application servers, database servers, and monitoring systems. === '''Management VLANs''' === Management VLANs are used exclusively for: * Network switches * Firewalls * Access points * Hypervisors and out‑of‑band management ports Access is restricted to IT administrators. === '''Voice VLANs''' === Voice VLANs are dedicated to VoIP devices such as desk phones. These VLANs: * Prioritize traffic for call quality (QoS) * Are isolated from user workstation VLANs * Allow communication only with VoIP backend systems === '''IoT and device VLANs''' === IoT VLANs contain low‑trust devices such as: * Printers * Cameras * Smart devices * Door access systems These VLANs have limited access to ensure a compromised IoT device cannot threaten core systems. ----- == '''VLAN numbering scheme''' == VLAN numbers follow a structured convention to simplify management: 1xx = User VLANs 2xx = Server VLANs 3xx = Management VLANs 4xx = Voice VLANs 5xx = IoT and device VLANs 9xx = Special-purpose or temporary VLANs Each VLAN name includes a descriptive label.
Example: 201-servers-app 315-mgmt-hypervisors 421-voice-floor1 504-iot-printers ----- == '''Inter‑VLAN routing''' == Routing between VLANs is controlled by the core switch or firewall.
Only explicitly defined routes are permitted based on: * Security requirements * Business needs * Application dependencies All inter‑VLAN communication must pass through firewall policies to ensure monitoring and auditing. ----- == '''VLAN lifecycle and maintenance''' == To ensure VLANs remain clean, consistent, and secure, the IT department performs: * '''Quarterly reviews''' to remove unused or deprecated VLANs * '''Documentation updates''' when adding or modifying VLANs * '''Security audits''' to ensure VLAN rules align with company standards * '''Capacity checks''' for IP addressing, DHCP scopes, and routing tables Temporary VLANs must include expiration dates and are removed when no longer required.

__TOC__ == '''Overview''' == This document outlines the standards and guidelines for creating, reviewing, and maintaining firewall rules within the company network. The purpose is to ensure consistent, secure, and auditable configuration of inbound and outbound network traffic. ----- == '''General principles''' == All firewall rules must follow these baseline principles: * '''Least privilege''' – Only the minimum required ports, protocols, and IP ranges should be allowed. * '''Deny by default''' – All traffic is denied unless explicitly approved. * '''Documented justification''' – Every rule must have a business or technical justification. * '''Owner assignment''' – Each rule must have a designated system or application owner. * '''Review lifecycle''' – Rules must be reviewed on a scheduled basis to ensure continued relevance and security. ----- == '''Rule types''' == Firewall rules fall into the following categories: === '''Inbound rules''' === Inbound rules control traffic originating from outside the network. These must be: * Highly restricted * Limited to specific services (e.g., HTTPS, VPN) * Bound to known IP ranges when possible * Logged for monitoring and auditing === '''Outbound rules''' === Outbound rules control traffic from inside the network to external destinations. These must be: * Defined per application or service * Restricted from accessing unnecessary external endpoints * Evaluated for potential data‑exfiltration risks === '''Internal rules''' === Internal rules define communication between systems within the corporate network, such as: * Application‑to‑database traffic * Service communications (APIs, message queues) * Backup and monitoring systems These rules must be segmented according to internal security zones. ----- == '''Rule request process''' == Requests for new firewall rules or modifications must follow the IT department’s change process: # '''Submit request''' via the IT support portal. # '''Provide full details''', including: #* Business justification #* Source and destination IPs #* Required ports and protocols #* Associated service or application # '''Security review''' performed by IT security. # '''Risk assessment''', ensuring compliance with internal security standards. # '''Implementation''' by the network team after approval. # '''Verification''', ensuring the rule functions as intended. Incomplete or unjustified requests will be rejected. ----- == '''Rule naming standards''' == To ensure clarity and maintainability, all firewall rules must follow the naming convention: ---- Example: webapp-internal-appsrv-dbsrv-1433 This structure helps with auditing, troubleshooting, and lifecycle management. ----- == '''Rule review and cleanup''' == Firewall rules must undergo periodic review to ensure they remain necessary and secure. * '''Quarterly review''' – All rules must be reviewed by rule owners and IT security. * '''Expiration policy''' – Temporary rules must include an expiration date and are removed automatically. * '''Removal procedure''' – Unused, outdated, or unjustified rules are removed after consultation with the system owner. * '''Logging and auditing''' – Changes must be logged and remain accessible for compliance checks. ----- == '''Compliance and enforcement''' == The IT department enforces firewall rule policies to maintain network security. * Non‑compliant rules may be disabled or removed. * Unauthorized changes are prohibited and subject to internal policy review. * Regular audits ensure adherence to security standards and regulatory requirements.

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 05 June 2023 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="dd7e91c6-3e1a-4664-816e-913d4d379afd" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="2b95241e-3c4a-4e97-85a1-52e525a82d59" ! ac:local-id="e3b23944-c51c-4935-873d-5ca0f7c07b73"| '''Action''' ! ac:local-id="e682fa7c-5bf6-4fd3-8a2f-df47695fdabd"| '''Owner''' ! ac:local-id="86811d79-cc87-4f89-b9ba-ad935dbcf759"| '''Deadline''' ! ac:local-id="5931a9c2-c4ed-40e1-af1e-41374e9be54b"| '''Status''' |- ac:local-id="e3f89ae5-a33c-4a57-84c1-e7ff2da53d7d" | ac:local-id="29ec7243-6277-4118-bfd3-ab0ba7456748"| Send weekly recruitment status updates to hiring managers | ac:local-id="91291089-1aa5-4cf7-a0a7-8b344a0030cc"| MK | ac:local-id="99e1e61d-7cf2-40bc-8cdb-85adf0accef6"| Starting next Monday | ac:local-id="8c5e02b2-d47d-4527-92ba-c35cb585952e"| Planned |- ac:local-id="9686f1ff23ab" | ac:local-id="f6c87539ed6a"| Draft standardized interview feedback template (Confluence / ATS) | ac:local-id="36e48c654e58"| MK | ac:local-id="07efaa0c9b0b"| 28 Mar | ac:local-id="75ac5bca4fcb"| in Progress |- ac:local-id="5f37cbd3629d" | ac:local-id="54acffeb27e0"| Design pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding plan | ac:local-id="46fb46119c32"| LF | ac:local-id="b6659a62cf10"| DW | ac:local-id="09a0f312f584"| 25 Mar | ac:local-id="9d75de32f3e5"| Planned |- ac:local-id="1d0c8cee6a05" | ac:local-id="43c46b18f57f"| Prepare communication plan for Employee Engagement Survey | ac:local-id="5308f2a862f3"| AS | ac:local-id="50acc1e692de"| 10 Apr | ac:local-id="3e2723fd8a4a"| In Progress |- ac:local-id="b0f89a6fe194" | ac:local-id="349af9354a7c"| Draft concept and agenda for first Quarterly Learning Day | ac:local-id="a96b63fd4c90"| DW | ac:local-id="51682a799aae"| 31 Mar | ac:local-id="f78ee64367db"| Not Started |- ac:local-id="379a4c665303" | ac:local-id="0aef6b74e314"| Review onboarding request process with IT to reduce tool access delays | ac:local-id="ab2e5dcca0fe"| LF | ac:local-id="f737ac88f437"| 15 Apr | ac:local-id="6bc7f64758a5"| Planned |- ac:local-id="b4c071dcea33" | ac:local-id="73a9ee9c0a87"| Research potential onboarding success metrics and reporting approach | ac:local-id="05ad1d9b1730"| DW | ac:local-id="c386305d9619"| 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

== Overview == The DEI (Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion) Council is a cross-functional group of employees dedicated to fostering an inclusive workplace culture. The Council works in partnership with HR and leadership to drive DEI initiatives, provide strategic input, and ensure accountability across the organization. == Objectives == * Promote a diverse and inclusive workplace * Advise on DEI strategy and initiatives * Amplify employee voices across different communities * Support equitable policies and practices == Council Structure == '''Composition:''' * Representatives from different departments and regions * Mix of roles, seniority levels, and backgrounds * Sponsored by an executive leader '''Term Length:''' * Members serve a 12-month term * Option to renew for an additional term == Key Responsibilities == * Provide feedback on policies, programs, and communications * Identify opportunities to improve inclusion and equity * Partner with HR on DEI initiatives and campaigns * Support internal events and awareness activities * Act as ambassadors for inclusive behaviors == Meeting Cadence == * Monthly Council meetings * Quarterly alignment with executive sponsors * Ad hoc working groups for specific initiatives == How to Join == # Open call for applications announced annually # Submit application including motivation and areas of interest # Selection based on diversity of perspectives and representation # Onboarding and orientation session for new members == Current Focus Areas == * Inclusive leadership practices * Gender equity and representation * Accessibility improvements * Cultural awareness and education == Success Metrics == * Employee perception of inclusion (survey results) * Participation in DEI initiatives * Representation across levels and functions * Implementation of Council recommendations == FAQ == '''Q: Do I need prior DEI experience to join?'''
No, passion and commitment to inclusion are the key criteria. '''Q: How much time is required?'''
Approximately 3–5 hours per month. __NOTOC__

5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

In 2026, marketing sets the direction for how we position, promote, and support our knowledge‑driven product ecosystem throughout the year. With four tightly connected products—'''PowerKnow''', '''InsightHub''', '''TaskRules''', and '''FlowChat'''—our focus is on delivering clear messaging, consistent storytelling, and campaigns that highlight the value of a unified knowledge experience. __TOC__ == Strategic priorities == Our marketing work in 2026 centers around four core priorities designed to strengthen brand cohesion and accelerate product adoption: * '''unified ecosystem narrative''': communicate how conversations, documentation, workflows, and expert insights connect across products * '''customer‑centric storytelling''': highlight real use cases, customer outcomes, and cross‑product value * '''scalable campaign frameworks''': create reusable assets and messaging that support quarterly feature launches * '''data‑driven decision making''': use analytics to refine targeting, content performance, and campaign timing These priorities guide all planning and execution throughout the year. == Key initiatives == To support our strategic priorities, the marketing team will focus on several major initiatives: * '''annual strategy & budget planning''': defining goals, allocating resources, and aligning with product roadmaps * '''feature launch campaigns''': coordinating quarterly releases across all four products * '''case studies and storytelling''': producing narratives that demonstrate real‑world impact * '''cross‑product messaging''': ensuring consistent language, visuals, and positioning across all channels * '''internal enablement''': equipping sales, support, and product teams with clear, up‑to‑date materials These initiatives ensure that marketing remains aligned with company objectives and product evolution. == Connected pages == The following subpages provide detailed plans, campaigns, and examples that support the 2026 marketing program: * '''Annual Marketing Strategy & Budget Planning''' Defines goals, priorities, and resource allocation for the year. * '''Product Feature Launch Campaigns''' Outlines quarterly launch activities for PowerKnow, InsightHub, TaskRules, and FlowChat. * '''Case Study: FlowChat Release''' Demonstrates how FlowChat supported a successful internal product launch. These pages work together to form a complete view of our marketing approach for 2026. == Conclusion == Marketing 2026 is about clarity, consistency, and connection. By aligning our campaigns, messaging, and storytelling across all products, we strengthen our brand and deliver a cohesive experience to customers. This space provides the foundation for planning, executing, and refining our work throughout the year. == See also == {{SubpageList|allChildren=true|page=}}

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 06 October 2025 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" __TOC__ == Overview == This article explains how employees can request new hardware, software, or upgrades through the company’s standard IT request workflow. Following the correct process ensures requests are approved, tracked, and fulfilled efficiently. == What you can request == Employees may request: * '''Hardware:''' laptops, monitors, keyboards, mice, docking stations, headsets, mobile devices * '''Software:''' productivity tools, development applications, licensed programs, upgrades, plug‑ins * '''Accessories:''' cables, adapters, chargers, peripheral devices Some items require manager approval or budget review. ----- == Submitting a hardware or software request == === 1. Go to the helpdesk portal === Open your browser and navigate to:
https://support.<yourcompany>.com === 2. Sign in === Use your company email and password. === 3. Select the request type === Choose '''Hardware request''' or '''Software request''' from the portal’s main menu or request catalog. === 4. Fill out the request form === Provide the required details: * '''Item requested:''' model, version, or description * '''Reason for request:''' replacement, new hire, upgrade, damaged item, required for project * '''Urgency:''' standard or high priority * '''Cost center (if applicable):''' where the expense should be charged * '''Manager approval:''' name of your manager Including clear details reduces delays. === 5. Submit the request === Click '''Submit'''.
You will receive a confirmation email with your request number. ----- == Approval process == === Manager approval === Most hardware and licensed software requests require manager approval.
Your manager will receive an approval notification. === IT review === After approval, the IT team will: * Verify compatibility * Check license or hardware availability * Confirm security requirements * Recommend alternatives if necessary === Procurement (if needed) === If the item is not in stock, IT will forward the request to procurement. Delivery times vary depending on vendor and availability. ----- == Receiving your hardware or software == === Hardware === Once approved and available, IT will: * Prepare or configure the device * Install required software * Notify you when it’s ready for pickup or delivery You may need to return old or broken equipment before receiving replacements. === Software === For licensed software, IT will: * Assign the license to your account * Install the software remotely or provide installation instructions * Confirm activation and functionality For SaaS (cloud‑based) applications, you may receive an email invitation or login credentials. ----- == Tracking your request == To check the status: # Sign in to the helpdesk portal. # Go to '''My requests'''. # View progress (Submitted, Awaiting approval, In progress, Completed). # Add comments or attachments if IT requests additional details. ----- == Troubleshooting or delays == === Missing approvals === If your manager has not approved the request, send a reminder or contact them directly. === Item out of stock === Some hardware may be back‑ordered. IT will update the request with estimated delivery times. === License limitations === If all licenses are currently in use, IT may propose: * Waiting for a license to become available * Purchasing additional licenses * Using an approved alternative application

DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities.
