Planned |- ac:local-id="b4c071dcea33" | ac:local-id="73a9ee9c0a87"| Research potential onboarding success metrics and reporting approach | ac:local-id="05ad1d9b1730"| DW | ac:local-id="c386305d9619"| 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 23 October 2023 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

__TOC__ == Overview == This article explains how to request IT support for company events, including meetings, workshops, training sessions, and all‑hands gatherings. Proper coordination helps ensure smooth audio/video performance, reliable connectivity, and on‑time setup. ----- == Types of events IT can support == IT can assist with: * On‑site or hybrid meetings * All‑hands or town hall events * Training sessions and workshops * Webinars and live streams * Conference room setup * Temporary equipment needs * Technical checks before guest presentations Support availability may depend on staffing and scheduling. ----- == When to request support == To ensure availability, submit your event request: * '''At least 5 business days in advance''' for standard meetings * '''At least 10 business days in advance''' for all‑hands or large events * '''As early as possible''' for external guest speakers or special requirements Last‑minute requests will be handled on a best‑effort basis. ----- == Submitting an event support request == === 1. Go to the helpdesk portal === Open your browser and navigate to:
https://support.<yourcompany>.com === 2. Sign in === Use your company email and password. === 3. Select “Event support request” === This is located under the '''Events & Meetings''' section or the general '''Request catalog'''. === 4. Provide event details === Fill out the form with: * '''Event name''' (e.g., “Q2 All‑Hands,” “Marketing Workshop”) * '''Date and time''' of the event * '''Location''' (conference room, auditorium, online, hybrid) * '''Number of participants''' (in‑person and virtual) * '''Technical requirements''', such as: ** Projector or large display ** Video conferencing setup ** Microphones (handheld, lapel, conference mics) ** Speakers or sound system ** Recording or live streaming ** Temporary Wi‑Fi or hotspot request * '''Any external participants''' or guest speakers * '''Point of contact''' during the event === 5. Submit the request === You will receive a confirmation email with your ticket number. ----- == What IT will do == === Before the event === IT may: * Review technical requirements * Reserve equipment * Schedule onsite support staff * Coordinate with facilities if needed * Perform a pre‑event technical check * Assist with meeting platform setup (Teams, Zoom, etc.) === During the event === For supported events, IT can: * Set up audio/video equipment * Assist speakers with presentations * Monitor hybrid/virtual connections * Troubleshoot issues in real time * Provide backup equipment if needed === After the event === IT may: * Power down and collect equipment * Save or upload recordings (if requested) * Log any follow‑up actions ----- == Large events and special considerations == === All‑hands or town halls === These may require: * Multiple microphones * Recording or streaming * Technician presence throughout * Rehearsal time * Additional equipment reservations === External guest speakers === IT may need: * Compatibility checks for laptops and video formats * Guest Wi‑Fi access * Temporary accounts for conferencing platforms === Hybrid events === For hybrid events, IT typically supports: * Camera and microphone setup * Testing of virtual meeting links * Conference room audio balancing * Display screen or projector alignment ----- == Troubleshooting common event issues == === Presentation not displaying === Try: * Reconnecting the HDMI/USB‑C cable * Using the “Duplicate screen” option * Restarting the presentation app (PowerPoint, Keynote) === Microphone not working === Check: * Battery levels * Input source on the room system * Mute switch or button === Participants cannot hear remote attendees === Ensure: * The correct input and output devices are selected * The conferencing app has microphone permissions * Audio is not routed through muted speakers === Wi‑Fi performance issues === If the network is congested, IT may: * Set up temporary access points * Provide wired connections * Recommend alternative meeting solutions

== Overview == The Team Engagement Activities program focuses on building a positive, inclusive, and collaborative workplace culture through structured and informal activities. These initiatives aim to strengthen relationships, improve communication, and enhance overall employee experience. == Objectives == * Strengthen team cohesion and collaboration * Improve employee morale and well-being * Encourage cross-functional interaction * Support an inclusive and engaging work environment == Activity Types == === Team-Building Events === Organized activities designed to improve collaboration and trust. '''Examples:''' * Workshops and problem-solving challenges * Offsite retreats * Escape rooms or group activities === Social Events === Informal gatherings that promote connection and relaxation. '''Examples:''' * Team lunches or dinners * Seasonal celebrations (e.g., summer events, year-end parties) * Coffee chats or virtual hangouts === Well-being Initiatives === Activities focused on physical and mental health. '''Examples:''' * Fitness challenges * Mindfulness or stress management sessions * Wellness days or guest speakers === Learning & Engagement Sessions === Interactive sessions that combine learning with engagement. '''Examples:''' * Lunch & Learn sessions * Internal knowledge-sharing talks * Guest speaker events == Participation Guidelines == * Activities are open to all team members unless otherwise specified * Participation is voluntary but encouraged * Managers should ensure inclusive access for remote and hybrid employees == Planning & Budget == '''Team-Level Activities:''' * Quarterly budget allocated per team * Organized by team leads or engagement champions '''Company-Wide Activities:''' * Coordinated by HR or Internal Communications * Larger events planned annually or bi-annually == Roles & Responsibilities == '''HR / People Team:''' * Provide frameworks and budget guidelines * Organize company-wide events * Monitor participation and feedback '''Managers:''' * Encourage participation * Support planning of team activities * Ensure inclusivity '''Employees:''' * Actively participate * Provide feedback and ideas * Respect inclusive and professional conduct == Feedback & Continuous Improvement == * Post-event surveys * Quarterly engagement pulse checks * Open feedback channels for suggestions == Success Metrics == * Participation rates in activities * Employee engagement survey scores * Feedback ratings on events * Cross-team collaboration indicators == Example Annual Calendar == '''Q1:''' Kick-off event + wellness challenge
'''Q2:''' Team-building workshops + social events
'''Q3:''' Summer event + learning series
'''Q4:''' Year-end celebration + recognition integration == FAQ == '''Q: Are remote employees included?'''
Yes, virtual and hybrid-friendly options are included in all planning. '''Q: Can employees suggest activities?'''
Absolutely—suggestions can be submitted via the Engagement Portal or directly to HR. __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 10 June 2024 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

__TOC__ == Overview == This article explains how employees can borrow IT equipment for short‑term use, including available items, eligibility, request procedures, loan duration, and return requirements. This process ensures that equipment is tracked, maintained, and available to all employees when needed. ----- == Available equipment == Common items available for short‑term checkout include: * Laptops * Projectors * Monitors * Headsets * Webcams * Keyboards and mice * Mobile hotspots * Presentation clickers * Specialized adapters or cables Availability may vary based on inventory. ----- == Eligibility == Employees may borrow equipment for: * Business travel * Presentations or meetings * Temporary project needs * Device repairs or replacements * Short‑term remote work Some items may require manager approval or justification due to cost or limited stock. ----- == Submitting a borrowing request == === 1. Go to the helpdesk portal === Navigate to:
https://support.<yourcompany>.com === 2. Sign in === Use your company email and password. === 3. Select “IT equipment borrowing” === This option is typically located under '''Hardware''' or '''Loaner equipment''' in the request catalog. === 4. Complete the request form === Provide: * '''Type of equipment needed''' * '''Reason for borrowing''' * '''Start date and expected return date''' * '''Location or pickup preference''' * '''Manager approval''', if required === 5. Submit the request === A confirmation email with your request number will be sent after submission. ----- == Loan duration == Standard loan periods: * '''1–14 days''' for most items * '''Up to 30 days''' with manager approval * '''Extended loans''' may be granted for long‑term projects, depending on availability Loan extensions must be requested through the helpdesk before the return date. ----- == Picking up the equipment == Once your request is approved and the item is ready, IT will notify you. Equipment must typically be picked up from: * The IT helpdesk counter * A designated equipment storage room * A scheduled delivery area (if applicable) You may need to sign a checkout form acknowledging responsibility. ----- == Returning borrowed equipment == === When to return === Equipment must be returned: * On or before the return date * Immediately if no longer needed * Before leaving the company === How to return === Bring the equipment to the IT helpdesk location and check it in with an IT staff member. They will: * Inspect the equipment * Verify all accessories are included (chargers, cables, cases, etc.) * Log the return in the tracking system === Late returns === If you miss the return date, IT may: * Send reminder notifications * Request manager follow‑up * Deny future loaner requests until items are returned ----- == Lost or damaged equipment == If equipment is lost, stolen, or damaged: # Notify the IT helpdesk immediately. # Provide details of the incident. # IT will coordinate repair or replacement. Depending on the situation, a cost center or manager may need to approve replacement charges. ----- == Tips for responsible use == * Bring all chargers and accessories back with the equipment. * Avoid installing unauthorized software. * Keep devices in secure locations—do not leave them in cars or public spaces. * Report any issues or malfunctions as soon as possible.

__TOC__ == Overview == This article explains how to reset your company password if you forgot it or if your account becomes locked due to too many failed login attempts. Follow the steps below based on your situation. == When to reset your password == You should reset your password if: * You forgot your current password * Your password expired * You receive an “invalid credentials” error * You suspect your account was compromised == When your account becomes locked == Your account may automatically lock after several failed login attempts. This is a security measure. You can unlock it by following the password‑reset process. == Resetting your password == === 1. Go to the password reset page === Open your browser and navigate to the password reset link:
https://portal.<yourcompany>.com/passwordreset === 2. Verify your identity === Enter your company email address.
You may be asked to complete one or more verification steps: * Approve a notification in your authentication app * Enter a verification code sent via SMS or email * Answer preconfigured security questions === 3. Create a new password === Follow the password requirements shown on the page.
Strong passwords typically include: * At least 12 characters * Uppercase and lowercase letters * Numbers * Special characters Avoid using personal information like names, birthdays, or common words. === 4. Confirm your new password === Re‑enter the password to confirm it. After submitting, you should see a confirmation that your password has been updated. == Unlocking your account == If your account is locked: # Follow the password reset procedure above. # Once your password is successfully changed, your account will automatically unlock. # Wait 2–5 minutes before attempting to log in again. == Troubleshooting == === You did not receive the verification code === * Check your spam or junk folder. * Ensure your phone has signal if you're expecting an SMS. * Verify that your MFA device is registered correctly. === Your MFA device is unavailable === Contact the IT helpdesk to temporarily disable MFA or register a new device. === You continue receiving lockout messages === Wait at least 10 minutes without attempting additional logins, then reset your password again.

== Overview == This page provides an overview of '''HR meeting minutes and documentation'''.
All HR meeting notes are stored as child pages under '''HR Minutes''' and organized by year. Use the page tree below to navigate to previous meeting notes. == Create New Meeting Minutes == To create a new set of HR meeting minutes, please use the '''HR Minutes Template''' located at the top of the page tree. '''Steps:''' # Open the page '''“HR Minutes Template”''' (first page in the list below). # Click the '''••• (More actions)''' menu in the top right. # Select '''Copy'''. # Rename the page using the format: '''Jour Fixe mm-dd-yyyy''' # Save the page as the '''latest minutes at the very top underneath the template''' within '''HR Minutes'''. == HR Minutes Navigation == This shows all meeting minutes: {{PageTree|content-title={{FULLPAGENAME}}}} == Quick Guidelines for Note Takers == When documenting HR meetings: * Record '''key discussion points''', not full transcripts. * Clearly highlight '''decisions made during the meeting'''. * Capture '''action items with owners and deadlines'''. * Use bullet points for readability. Recommended sections in meeting notes: * Participants * Agenda * Discussion * Key Points * Decisions * Action Items == Related Pages == * [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Policies|HR Policies]] * [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes|HR Processes]] * [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Initiatives|HR Initiatives]] __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 10 November 2025 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 09 October 2023 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant
