== Overview == Evaluation criteria provide a structured way to assess candidates across multiple dimensions. Using consistent criteria helps reduce bias and ensures alignment with role expectations. == Key Dimensions == * '''Technical / Functional Skills''' ** Mastery of required tools, languages, or methods ** Problem-solving and analytical ability ** Practical assessment outcomes (e.g., test results, case studies) * '''Cultural Fit / Values Alignment''' ** Demonstrates behaviors aligned with Averion Solutions’ values ** Collaboration, accountability, and growth mindset ** Adaptability and openness to feedback * '''Communication & Collaboration''' ** Clarity and structure in presenting ideas ** Ability to work in a team and handle conflict ** Responsiveness and interpersonal skills * '''Potential for Growth''' ** Willingness to learn and take on new responsibilities ** Career trajectory alignment with role opportunities ** Leadership potential (if applicable) === Scoring System (Example) === * '''5''' – Excellent: exceeds expectations * '''4''' – Strong: meets and slightly exceeds expectations * '''3''' – Satisfactory: meets expectations * '''2''' – Needs Improvement: partially meets expectations * '''1''' – Unsatisfactory: does not meet expectations '''Tip:''' Use a consistent scoring scale across all interviewers to maintain fairness. == Notes == * Scores should be backed by examples or evidence * Avoid subjective statements like “I just feel they are a good fit” without supporting observations * Some roles may include additional dimensions (e.g., leadership, client management) __NOTOC__

__TOC__ == Overview == This article explains how to connect your company laptop or mobile device to Wi‑Fi networks, including office Wi‑Fi, home Wi‑Fi, and public networks. It also includes troubleshooting tips for common connection issues. == Before you begin == Make sure you have: * Your device’s Wi‑Fi switched on * The correct network name (SSID) * The Wi‑Fi password, if required * Access permissions, if connecting to a company‑secured network If you do not have the required Wi‑Fi credentials for office networks, contact the IT helpdesk. == Connecting on Windows == === 1. Open Wi‑Fi settings === Click the '''network icon''' in the taskbar (bottom‑right corner).
Select '''Wi‑Fi''', then click '''Show available networks'''. === 2. Choose the network === Select the Wi‑Fi network you want to connect to. === 3. Enter the password === If prompted, enter the Wi‑Fi password and click '''Next'''. === 4. Confirm connection === Once connected, the network icon will show full or partial signal strength bars. == Connecting on macOS == === 1. Open Wi‑Fi settings === Click the '''Wi‑Fi icon''' in the top‑right menu bar. === 2. Select the network === Choose your Wi‑Fi network from the list. === 3. Enter the password === Type the Wi‑Fi password and click '''Join'''. === 4. Verify connection === The Wi‑Fi icon will turn black (or show full bars) when successfully connected. == Connecting on mobile devices == === iOS (iPhone/iPad) === # Open '''Settings''' → '''Wi‑Fi'''. # Select the network. # Enter the password. # Confirm the connection checkmark appears. === Android === # Open '''Settings''' → '''Network & Internet''' → '''Wi‑Fi'''. # Choose the network. # Enter the password. # Tap '''Connect'''. == Troubleshooting == === You cannot see the Wi‑Fi network === Try: * Moving closer to the access point * Enabling Wi‑Fi on your device * Restarting your device * Ensuring airplane mode is off If you still don’t see office Wi‑Fi, contact IT to check access permissions. === You receive “incorrect password” === Confirm that: * You typed the password correctly * You are connecting to the correct SSID * Caps lock is not on If the password was recently changed, request the updated one. === You are connected but have no internet === Try: * Disconnecting and reconnecting * Restarting your device * Forgetting the network, then reconnecting * Switching to another Wi‑Fi network This issue may also occur temporarily due to network maintenance. === Your device keeps disconnecting === Check: * The strength of your Wi‑Fi signal * Interference from nearby devices * Whether other devices can connect normally If only your device disconnects, it may need a network driver or OS update. === You cannot connect to secure office Wi‑Fi === Some company networks require: * Device registration * Up‑to‑date antivirus * Updated operating system * Configuration profiles Contact IT if you suspect your device does not meet the requirements.

== Overview == This checklist outlines all required information and actions needed to submit a hiring request for approval. Completing this checklist helps ensure a smooth and timely approval process. == Pre-Submission Checklist == === Role Definition === ☐ Define business justification for the role
☐ Confirm whether role is new or replacement
☐ Define key responsibilities
☐ Identify required skills and experience
☐ Determine employment type (full-time, part-time, contract)
☐ Define reporting line and manager === Budget & Approval Alignment === ☐ Confirm headcount availability
☐ Align with Finance on budget
☐ Define salary range
☐ Validate against internal salary bands === Documentation === ☐ Prepare draft job description
☐ Outline expected start date
☐ Identify business priority level === Submission === ☐ Submit hiring request to HR
☐ Notify relevant stakeholders (if required) == Post-Submission (Tracking) == ☐ HR review completed
☐ Feedback incorporated (if applicable)
☐ Final approval received
☐ Role marked as approved == Tips for Faster Approval == * Provide clear and specific business justification * Align with Finance before submission * Avoid submitting incomplete requests * Anticipate questions from HR and leadership == Common Issues == '''Incomplete information'''
→ Leads to back-and-forth and delays '''Unclear salary expectations'''
→ Requires revalidation with Finance '''Missing stakeholder alignment'''
→ Slows down approval process == Notes == * This checklist is intended as a guideline; additional requirements may apply depending on the role or department * Some teams use local templates in addition to this checklist __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 03 June 2024 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

{{Info |body = Not available in Confluence cloud editor. Migration example comes from the script. }} The macro showed '''read‑only metadata''' about the current space, typically including: === '''Space name''' === The human‑readable name of the space. === '''Space key''' === The short identifier (e.g., DEV, HR, DOC). === '''Space description''' === The description text configured in Space Settings. === '''Space type''' === * Global space * Personal space === '''Space category labels''' === If the space was assigned to categories, they appeared here. === '''Space administrators''' === A list of users/groups with admin permissions. === '''Creation date''' === When the space was originally created. === '''Last modified date''' === When the space’s content was last updated. === '''Permissions summary''' === A high‑level overview of who can view/edit/administer the space. === '''Storage usage (in some versions)''' === How much storage the space consumed. === '''Links to space tools''' === Quick links to: * Space permissions * Content tools * Look & feel * Integrations Not all versions showed all items, but this is the typical set. __NOTOC__

Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 19 January 2026 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 13 November 2023 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

== Overview == Offer approval ensures that all aspects of the proposed employment—salary, benefits, and terms—are compliant with company policies, internal pay structures, and budget constraints. == Approval Steps == * '''Prepare Draft Offer''' ** HR consolidates recommended salary, bonus, and benefits ** Hiring Manager reviews and confirms alignment with the role * '''Internal Reviews''' ** Finance confirms budget availability and alignment with salary bands ** Department Director or Head of HR provides final sign-off (for certain roles) * '''Final Approval''' ** Approved offer is ready to be communicated to the candidate == Roles & Responsibilities == * '''HR:''' Prepare offer, coordinate reviews, document approvals * '''Hiring Manager:''' Validate offer details, provide role-specific input * '''Finance:''' Confirm budget and internal equity * '''Department Leadership:''' Approve senior or strategic offers == Common Challenges == * Delays due to multiple approval layers * Misalignment between proposed compensation and internal guidelines * Last-minute changes requested by leadership == Notes == * Some roles allow for expedited approvals, especially in competitive markets * Documentation of approval steps is required for compliance and audit purposes __NOTOC__

== Overview == A clear and well-structured job description is a critical foundation for a successful hiring process. It ensures alignment between hiring managers, candidates, and stakeholders, and helps attract candidates who match both the technical and cultural requirements of the role. At Averion Solutions, job descriptions are created collaboratively between the Hiring Manager and HR. While the Hiring Manager defines the role requirements, HR ensures consistency, clarity, and alignment with internal standards. == Objectives of a Job Description == A strong job description should: * Clearly define the purpose of the role * Outline key responsibilities and expectations * Specify required and preferred qualifications * Reflect team context and ways of working * Support realistic candidate expectations Job descriptions also play an important role in ensuring consistency across roles and supporting fair hiring practices. == Process Overview == The job description creation process typically includes: # Drafting the job description (Hiring Manager) # Reviewing and refining content (HR) # Aligning on scope and expectations # Final approval before publishing '''See:''' [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Hiring_Workflow|Hiring Workflow]] == Key Components == A standard job description at Averion Solutions includes the following sections: === Role Overview === * Brief summary of the role * Context within the team or organization === Key Responsibilities === * Core tasks and areas of ownership * Day-to-day activities * Expected outcomes (where possible) === Required Qualifications === * Must-have skills and experience * Relevant education or certifications (if applicable) === Preferred Qualifications === * Additional skills that are beneficial but not required === Reporting Structure === * Direct manager * Team or department context === Work Setup === * Remote, hybrid, or on-site * Location requirements (if applicable) == Writing Guidelines == === Be Clear and Specific === Avoid vague statements such as: * “Handle various tasks” * “Work in a dynamic environment” Instead, describe concrete responsibilities and expectations. === Focus on Outcomes, Not Just Tasks === Where possible, describe what success looks like in the role rather than only listing activities. === Avoid Overloading Requirements === Excessively long requirement lists can discourage strong candidates from applying. '''Guideline:'''
Differentiate clearly between “required” and “nice-to-have”. === Use Inclusive Language === Ensure that wording is inclusive and does not unintentionally discourage certain candidate groups. * Avoid gender-coded language * Avoid unnecessary requirements * Focus on skills and potential === Align with Internal Role Levels === Job descriptions should be consistent with existing roles in terms of: * Scope * Seniority * Compensation range HR supports alignment with internal job frameworks where applicable. == Common Challenges == '''Role scope is too broad'''
→ Leads to unclear expectations and mismatched candidates '''Too many requirements'''
→ Reduces candidate pool '''Lack of alignment with stakeholders'''
→ Causes delays later in the process '''Generic descriptions'''
→ Attract less relevant candidates == Review & Approval == Before publishing, job descriptions are reviewed by HR to ensure: * Clarity and structure * Consistency with internal standards * Alignment with compensation and role levels In some cases, additional review may be required (e.g., for new roles or senior positions). == Template == The standard job description template should be used for all roles: [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Job_Description_Creation/Job_Description_Template|Job Description Template]] (''Note: Some teams maintain slightly modified versions of the template for specific roles.'') You can find some examples [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Job_Description_Creation/Job_Description_Examples|here]]. == Tips & Best Practices == * Reuse existing job descriptions where possible * Review similar roles before creating a new one * Involve team members for input on responsibilities * Keep descriptions updated for frequently hired roles == Notes == * Older job descriptions may not fully follow the current structure * Some departments use more detailed or role-specific formats * Updates to the template are made periodically but not always consistently adopted __NOTOC__

At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" Planned |- ac:local-id="b4c071dcea33" | ac:local-id="73a9ee9c0a87"| Research potential onboarding success metrics and reporting approach | ac:local-id="05ad1d9b1730"| DW | ac:local-id="c386305d9619"| 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 16 February 2026 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" __TOC__ == Overview == The company VPN (virtual private network) allows secure access to internal systems, shared drives, and applications when working remotely. This article explains how to install, configure, and connect to the VPN, along with solutions to common connection problems. == Before you begin == You will need: * Your company email and password * VPN client software (provided by IT) * A stable internet connection * MFA enabled, if required for VPN sign‑ins If you have not received the VPN setup package or instructions, contact the IT helpdesk. == Installing the VPN client == === Windows === # Download the installer from the company software portal. # Double‑click the installer and follow the on‑screen setup steps. # When prompted, approve the installation with your device’s admin permissions. === macOS === # Download the VPN client for macOS. # Open the .dmg file and drag the app into the '''Applications''' folder. # When opening the app for the first time, click '''Open''' if macOS shows a security prompt. === Mobile devices (optional) === Install the VPN app recommended by IT from the Apple App Store or Google Play Store, then sign in using your company credentials. == Setting up the VPN == === 1. Open the VPN client === Launch the VPN application from your computer or mobile device. === 2. Enter the server address === Type the VPN server address provided by IT (example):
vpn.<yourcompany>.com === 3. Sign in with your credentials === Enter your company email and password. === 4. Complete MFA (if required) === Follow the prompt to approve a sign‑in notification or enter your verification code. === 5. Connect to the VPN === Click '''Connect'''.
Once connected, you should see a green icon or message such as '''Connected''' or '''Secure'''. == Using the VPN == Once connected: * Company applications and internal sites should work normally. * Network drives or file shares will appear as they do in the office. * All traffic to internal systems is securely encrypted. To disconnect, open the VPN client and click '''Disconnect'''. == Common issues == === You cannot connect to the VPN === Check: * Your internet connection is working. * The VPN server address is correct. * You are using the latest version of the VPN client. Restarting your computer often resolves temporary connection problems. === You receive MFA errors === Try: * Opening your authenticator app manually. * Ensuring your phone has internet access. * Using a backup MFA method if available. If none work, contact IT to reset your MFA settings. === The VPN logs in but disconnects frequently === This is usually caused by unstable internet. Try: * Moving closer to your Wi‑Fi router. * Switching to a wired connection. * Restarting your router if possible. === You can connect but cannot access internal resources === Check: * Your VPN session shows a proper connection (green/check icon). * Your device firewall is not blocking VPN traffic. * You are connected to the correct VPN profile (some companies use multiple profiles). If still blocked, IT may need to update your access permissions. === You receive “username or password incorrect” === Try: * Logging into your company account in a browser to confirm your password. * Resetting your password if needed. * Waiting 5–10 minutes if your account was recently unlocked.

At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__
