__TOC__ == Overview == This article explains how to connect your device to company printers and how to resolve common printing and scanning issues. Instructions cover Windows, macOS, and multifunction printer (MFP) devices. == Before you begin == Make sure you have: * A network connection (Wi‑Fi or Ethernet) * Access permissions to company printers * The printer name or IP address (provided by IT) * Any required drivers for your device If you are unsure which printer to use, contact the IT helpdesk. ----- == Connecting to printers == == Windows == === 1. Open printer settings === Go to '''Start''' → '''Settings''' → '''Bluetooth & devices''' → '''Printers & scanners'''. === 2. Add a printer === Click '''Add device''' or '''Add a printer or scanner'''. === 3. Select the printer === Choose the correct printer from the list.
If it does not appear, click '''The printer that I want isn’t listed''', then: * Select '''Add a printer using a TCP/IP address or hostname''', and * Enter the printer’s IP address or network name. === 4. Install drivers === Windows will automatically install the drivers. If prompted, allow installation. === 5. Confirm connection === A test page can be printed to verify the printer is set up correctly. ----- == macOS == === 1. Open system settings === Go to '''System Settings''' → '''Printers & Scanners'''. === 2. Add printer === Click the '''+''' button. === 3. Select the printer === Choose the printer from the nearby devices list.
If it does not appear: * Click '''IP''', * Enter the printer’s IP address, * Choose '''Line Printer Daemon (LPD)''' or '''Internet Printing Protocol (IPP)'''. === 4. Install drivers === macOS may download drivers automatically or prompt you to install them. === 5. Confirm connection === Print a test page to ensure everything works correctly. ----- == Connecting to scanners == Most office MFPs allow scanning in three ways: === 1. Scan to email === Select '''Scan → Email''' on the printer touchscreen.
Enter your company email address, then press '''Start'''. === 2. Scan to network folder === Some printers support scanning directly to a shared company folder.
If this is enabled, IT will provide instructions. === 3. Scan from your computer === On Windows or macOS, use the built‑in '''Scan''' app or the MFP’s vendor software.
Make sure the printer is installed and visible in your device’s scanner list. ----- == Printer troubleshooting == === Printing is stuck or won’t start === Try: * Canceling all print jobs * Restarting your computer * Restarting the printer (if allowed) * Reconnecting to Wi‑Fi or Ethernet === Printer not appearing on the network === Check: * You are connected to the correct Wi‑Fi/VPN * The printer is powered on * The printer has network connectivity (most devices show a network icon) If using VPN, some companies require VPN to access network printers. === “Offline” status === Try: * Removing and re‑adding the printer * Switching networks (from Wi‑Fi to Ethernet) * Restarting your computer === Paper jams === Follow the on‑device touchscreen instructions: * Open the indicated panel * Remove any stuck sheets carefully * Close all panels fully before retrying ----- == Scanner troubleshooting == === Scanner not detected by your computer === Check: * The printer is installed as both a '''printer''' and a '''scanner''' * You are on the same network * You updated your drivers Try reinstalling the printer if the scanner component is missing. === “Scan failed” errors === This often occurs when: * Your Wi‑Fi connection is unstable * The file size is too large * The scanner is busy with another job Try scanning at a lower resolution or waiting a few minutes. === Scans not arriving in email === Check: * Your email inbox and spam folder * That your mailbox is not full * The printer is configured with correct SMTP settings (IT handles this)

== Overview == Averion Solutions prioritizes '''mental and emotional wellbeing''' as a core component of employee health. Our Mental Health & Mindfulness program is designed to help employees '''manage stress, build resilience, and foster focus and clarity'''. Through a combination of structured workshops, digital resources, and everyday mindfulness practices, we aim to create a supportive environment that encourages '''self-care, emotional intelligence, and psychological safety'''. == Program Components == === Mindfulness Workshops === * Guided meditation sessions, breathing exercises, and stress-reduction techniques. * Led by certified mindfulness coaches or wellness specialists. * Available both on-site and virtually to accommodate hybrid or remote employees. === Mental Health Resources === * Access to confidential counseling services and Employee Assistance Programs (EAP). * Digital resources including webinars, articles, and mental health toolkits. * Resources on coping strategies for stress, burnout, and work-life balance. === Mental Fitness Challenges === * Initiatives such as gratitude journaling, daily mindfulness check-ins, or focus exercises. * Optional participation in team challenges to encourage shared wellbeing practices. * Recognition or small incentives for consistent engagement. === Manager-Led Support === * Training for managers on spotting signs of stress or burnout. * Encouraging regular check-ins and empathetic conversations. * Fostering a culture where mental health discussions are normalized and stigma-free. === Wellbeing Days & Flexibility === * Access to designated mental wellness days or flexible time off to recharge. * Encouragement of short daily breaks for reflection or mindfulness exercises. == Employee Resources == * '''Meditation Library:''' Short guided sessions for focus, relaxation, and stress relief. * '''Mental Health Articles & Videos:''' Practical strategies for managing workload and emotions. * '''Wellbeing Apps & Subscriptions:''' Access to mindfulness and mental health apps. * '''Support Contacts:''' HR and EAP contacts for confidential guidance. {{Tip |body = == Participation Tips == * Dedicate '''5–10 minutes daily''' for mindfulness exercises. * Join workshops or virtual sessions to learn structured techniques. * Keep a journal to track stress levels, mood, or coping strategies. * Encourage team participation for shared mental health practices. }} __NOTOC__

At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" == Overview == The Inclusive Hiring Guidelines provide a structured approach to ensure fair, unbiased, and equitable recruitment practices. These guidelines are designed to help attract diverse talent and create a consistent, inclusive candidate experience across all stages of the hiring process. == Objectives == * Reduce bias in hiring decisions * Increase diversity in candidate pipelines * Ensure fair and consistent evaluation practices * Promote an inclusive employer brand == Key Principles == * '''Fairness:''' All candidates are evaluated using consistent criteria * '''Transparency:''' Clear communication throughout the hiring process * '''Accessibility:''' Accommodations provided as needed * '''Representation:''' Diverse interview panels where possible == Hiring Process Guidelines == === Job Description Creation === * Use inclusive and gender-neutral language * Focus on essential skills rather than unnecessary requirements * Avoid jargon or biased phrasing === Sourcing & Outreach === * Use diverse sourcing channels and platforms * Partner with organizations and communities representing underrepresented groups * Encourage employee referrals from diverse networks === Screening & Shortlisting === * Use structured screening criteria * Avoid relying solely on educational background or specific employers * Consider blind screening where feasible === Interview Process === * Use structured interview questions aligned with competencies * Provide interviewer training on unconscious bias * Ensure diverse interview panels when possible === Decision & Offer === * Use standardized evaluation scorecards * Document hiring decisions * Conduct calibration discussions to ensure fairness == Roles & Responsibilities == '''Hiring Managers:''' * Define clear role requirements * Ensure structured and fair evaluation '''Recruiters:''' * Guide inclusive hiring practices * Monitor diversity of candidate pipelines '''Interviewers:''' * Complete bias awareness training * Follow structured interview approach == Tools & Resources == * Inclusive job description templates * Interview question banks * Bias awareness training modules * Candidate experience guidelines == Success Metrics == * Diversity of candidate pipelines * Interview-to-offer conversion rates * Candidate satisfaction scores * Hiring manager compliance with guidelines == FAQ == '''Q: Are diverse panels mandatory?'''
Strongly encouraged, but may vary depending on availability. '''Q: What if I need to hire quickly?'''
Inclusive practices should still be followed to ensure fairness. __NOTOC__

== Overview == The Employee Recognition and Award Nomination program is designed to celebrate individual and team contributions that align with our company values and business goals. The program promotes a culture of appreciation, reinforces positive behaviors, and recognizes outstanding performance across all levels of the organization. == Objectives == * Foster a culture of recognition and appreciation * Reinforce company values through visible behaviors * Increase employee engagement and motivation * Highlight achievements and contributions across teams == Recognition Types == === Peer-to-Peer Recognition === Employees can recognize colleagues for their contributions in real time. '''Features:''' * Instant recognition via the Recognition Platform * Public visibility within teams or company-wide * Option to include a short message and company value tag === Manager Recognition === Managers can formally acknowledge team members for exceptional performance. '''Examples:''' * Project completion recognition * Going above and beyond role expectations * Demonstrating leadership or innovation === Spot Awards === Ad hoc awards for outstanding contributions. '''Details:''' * Monetary or non-monetary rewards * No fixed nomination cycle * Requires manager approval === Annual Awards Program === A structured recognition initiative held once per year. '''Categories (examples):''' * Employee of the Year * Team Excellence Award * Innovation Award * Customer Impact Award * Leadership Award == Nomination Process == # Submit nomination via the Recognition Portal # Provide supporting details (impact, outcomes, alignment with values) # Manager review and endorsement (if applicable) # Evaluation by HR / Recognition Committee # Announcement and celebration == Eligibility == * All employees are eligible to give and receive recognition * Award nominations may require a minimum tenure (e.g., 6 months) * Self-nominations are not permitted for formal awards == Rewards & Incentives == * Monetary bonuses or gift vouchers * Digital badges and certificates * Public recognition in company meetings or internal channels * Feature stories on the intranet == Communication & Visibility == * Monthly recognition highlights shared company-wide * Dedicated space on the intranet for recognition posts * Recognition moments included in town halls and team meetings == Success Metrics == * Number of recognitions submitted per month * Participation rate across departments * Employee engagement survey results (recognition-related questions) * Retention rates of high-performing employees == FAQ == '''Q: Can I recognize someone outside my team?'''
Yes, cross-team recognition is strongly encouraged. '''Q: Are recognitions tied to performance reviews?'''
They may be considered as supporting input but are not the sole factor. __NOTOC__

__TOC__ == Overview == This article explains how to access your company account using your assigned credentials. You will need your company email address, password, and (if enabled) multi‑factor authentication (MFA). == Before you begin == Make sure you have: * Your company-issued email address * Your initial or current password * Access to your MFA method (mobile app, text message, or hardware token) If you haven’t received your login information, contact the IT helpdesk. == Steps to log in == === 1. Navigate to the login portal === Open your browser and go to the company login page:
https://portal.<yourcompany>.com === 2. Enter your email address === Type your full company email (for example, [mailto:firstname.lastname@yourcompany.com firstname.lastname@yourcompany.com]). === 3. Enter your password === Type your current password. * If this is your first login, you may be prompted to create a new password. === 4. Complete MFA (if required) === If multi‑factor authentication is enabled, follow the prompt to approve a sign‑in request or enter your verification code. === 5. Access your account === Click '''Sign in'''. If everything is correct, you’ll be redirected to your company dashboard. == Troubleshooting == === You forgot your password === Click '''Forgot password?''' and follow the instructions. A reset link will be sent to your recovery contact. === Your MFA method is unavailable === Contact the IT helpdesk to reset or update your MFA settings. === You receive an “invalid credentials” error === Double‑check spelling, especially in your email address. If it continues, perform a password reset.

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 17 July 2023 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

== '''Overview''' == At Averion Solutions, we believe that a healthy, resilient, and engaged workforce is the foundation of our success. The '''Wellbeing & Workplace Support''' programs are designed to help employees thrive physically, mentally, socially, and financially, while supporting work-life balance. These initiatives provide practical resources, guidance, and activities to support your overall wellbeing, both at work and beyond. == '''Wellbeing Pillars''' == * Physical Health & Fitness * Mental Health & Mindfulness * Nutrition & Healthy Eating * Work-Life Balance & Flexible Work * Social & Team Wellbeing * Financial Wellbeing == Programs == {{SubpageList|depth=2|allChildren=true|style=|sortAndReverse=|first=0|page=}} == '''How to Participate / Access Programs''' == * All programs are available to Averion Solutions employees. * Participation is voluntary. Many programs can be accessed online or in person. * For guidance on registration or accessing services, visit the individual program pages or contact the '''Wellbeing Coordinator'''. __NOTOC__

__TOC__ == Overview == Multi‑factor authentication (MFA) provides an additional layer of security for your company account. After entering your password, you’ll be asked to verify your identity using a secondary method such as an authentication app, text message, or hardware token.
This article explains how to set up MFA and how to resolve common issues. == Before you begin == You will need: * Your company email and password * A smartphone or other approved MFA device * The company‑approved authenticator app (commonly Microsoft Authenticator, Google Authenticator, or a similar tool) If you’re unsure which method your company uses, contact the IT helpdesk. == Setting up MFA == === 1. Sign in to the setup page === Go to:
https://portal.<yourcompany>.com/security Sign in with your company email and password. === 2. Select your verification method === Choose your preferred MFA method: * '''Authentication app (recommended)''' * '''Text message (SMS)''' * '''Phone call''' * '''Hardware token''' (if issued by IT) === 3. Set up the authentication app (recommended) === If you choose an app: # Install the company‑approved authentication app on your device. # On your computer, click '''Set up'''. # Scan the QR code displayed on your screen using the app. # Approve the test notification or enter the provided 6‑digit code. === 4. Set up SMS verification === If using SMS: # Enter your mobile phone number. # You’ll receive a 6‑digit code via text. # Enter the code on the setup page to confirm. === 5. Set up a hardware token === If your company provides a hardware token: # Press the button on the token to generate a one‑time code. # Enter the code on the setup page. # Repeat if prompted for verification. === 6. Complete setup === Once confirmed, your MFA method will be added to your security profile.
You may add multiple methods for backup. == Using MFA to sign in == After entering your password during login: * You will receive a push notification '''or''' * You will enter a 6‑digit verification code '''or''' * You will use your hardware token Follow the on‑screen instructions to complete authentication. == Troubleshooting == === You’re not receiving authentication app notifications === Try the following: * Open the authentication app manually and refresh. * Ensure your phone has internet access. * Check that notifications are enabled for the app. * Restart your device. === You’re not receiving SMS codes === Check: * Your phone has signal. * Your device isn’t blocking unknown senders. * You’re located in an area with cellular coverage. If still not working, switch to another MFA method if available. === Your phone was lost, replaced, or reset === You will need your MFA settings reset. Contact the IT helpdesk and request:
'''“MFA reset / clear registered authentication methods.”''' === You get an “authentication failed” message === This may occur if: * The code is expired (codes typically last 30 seconds). * You entered the wrong code. * Your time/date settings are incorrect. For authentication apps, enable '''automatic time sync''' on your phone. === Your hardware token is not working === Try: * Using a new code (press the button again). * Ensuring the token has not expired. * Requesting a replacement if the device is damaged.

== Overview == [[File:Migration_FlowChat_Portal-canva-product-FlowChat.png|250px|right|link=Migration:Products/FlowChat/FlowChat_Portal]] This case study highlights how the marketing team leveraged '''FlowChat''', our conversation‑to‑knowledge engine, during its latest product release cycle. FlowChat connects discussions to relevant [[Migration:Products/InsightHub/InsightHub_Portal|InsightHub]] articles and can even generate new knowledge items from marked answers. The result is a smoother launch process, faster content creation, and more consistent messaging across teams. == Challenge == Before FlowChat, the marketing team faced several recurring issues during release cycles: * scattered conversations across chat tools, email threads, and meetings * difficulty locating the latest product details or positioning statements * repeated questions about features, timelines, and messaging * inconsistent documentation quality and outdated internal references These challenges slowed down campaign planning and made it harder to maintain a unified brand voice. == Approach == During the FlowChat release, the marketing team adopted FlowChat internally as both a collaboration tool and a knowledge accelerator. The team integrated it into daily standups, content planning sessions, and cross‑functional discussions with product and support. Key practices included: * using FlowChat channels for all release‑related conversations * marking authoritative answers, allowing FlowChat to generate draft InsightHub articles * relying on FlowChat’s automatic suggestions to surface existing documentation * consolidating messaging, FAQs, and feature descriptions into a shared knowledge space This approach ensured that every conversation contributed to a growing, structured knowledge base. == Results == The FlowChat release demonstrated measurable improvements in the marketing workflow: * '''faster content creation''': draft blog posts, FAQs, and campaign briefs were generated directly from marked answers * '''consistent messaging''': suggested InsightHub articles ensured everyone referenced the same approved information * '''reduced duplication''': fewer repeated questions and less time spent searching for details * '''improved cross‑team alignment''': product, support, and marketing shared a single source of truth The team reported that FlowChat reduced preparation time for launch materials by more than half. == Lessons learned == The release provided several insights into how FlowChat can best support marketing teams: * structured conversations lead to higher‑quality knowledge items * marking answers early prevents confusion later in the release cycle * suggested articles help maintain consistency across all content formats * integrating FlowChat into existing workflows is more effective than introducing new channels These lessons will guide future releases and help refine FlowChat’s internal rollout strategy. == Conclusion == The FlowChat release showcased how powerful conversation‑driven knowledge creation can be. By turning everyday discussions into structured, reusable insights, the marketing team accelerated its work, improved alignment, and delivered a more cohesive launch. FlowChat proved not only to be a product worth promoting but also a tool that strengthens the company’s own processes. {{AttachmentsSectionStart}} * [[Media:Migration_Case_Study_FlowChat_Release-canva-product-FlowChat.png]] {{AttachmentsSectionEnd}} __NOTOC__

== Overview == The Hiring Decision Framework outlines how final hiring decisions are made after evaluation. It clarifies responsibilities, approvals, and tie-breaking rules. === Step 1: Review Evaluation Scores === * HR aggregates scores and feedback from all interviewers * Scores are compared against role benchmarks and internal standards === Step 2: Panel Discussion === * Hiring panel reviews aggregated scores * Strengths and weaknesses of candidates are discussed * Any discrepancies in scoring are addressed === Step 3: Final Recommendation === * Hiring Manager makes a recommendation based on evaluation and discussion * HR validates alignment with policies, budget, and role requirements === Step 4: Approval === '''Typical Approvers:''' * Hiring Manager (primary recommendation) * Department Director (for senior or critical roles) * HR (policy, fairness, compliance) '''Notes:''' * Some roles may require executive review * Urgent roles may use a streamlined approval process === Step 5: Offer or Rejection === * HR communicates decision to candidates * Feedback may be provided to rejected candidates (optional) * All documentation is stored in the applicant tracking system == Tie-Breaking Scenarios == If multiple candidates score equally, consider: * Alignment with team needs * Past experience and career trajectory * Potential for growth and development == Common Challenges == * Disagreement among interviewers * Pressure to hire quickly vs. waiting for the ideal candidate * Maintaining transparency and fairness == Notes == * All hiring decisions must be documented * Framework may be adapted for senior, high-impact, or strategic roles * Feedback loops are encouraged for continuous improvement __NOTOC__

== Overview == The Training and Certification Program is designed to support continuous learning and professional growth across the organization. It provides employees with access to structured learning paths, role-specific training, and industry-recognized certifications to enhance both individual performance and organizational capability. == Objectives == * Build critical skills aligned with business priorities * Support career development and internal mobility * Increase employee engagement and retention * Ensure compliance with industry standards and regulations == Program Structure == === Core Learning Paths === Each role family has a defined learning path that includes: * Foundational onboarding training * Intermediate skill development modules * Advanced specialization courses Example tracks: * Leadership Development * Project Management * Technical & Digital Skills * Compliance & Risk Awareness === Certification Support === Employees are encouraged to pursue external certifications relevant to their roles. '''Support includes:''' * Reimbursement of certification exam fees (up to €1,500 per year) * Paid study leave (up to 5 days per certification) * Access to preparation materials and practice exams '''Popular certifications (examples):''' * PMP (Project Management Professional) * Certified Scrum Master (CSM) * AWS / Azure certifications * CIPD HR certifications === Learning Formats === To accommodate different learning styles and schedules, the program offers: * Instructor-led workshops (virtual and in-person) * Self-paced e-learning modules * Microlearning sessions (15–30 minutes) * External conferences and seminars == Eligibility == * All full-time employees after 3 months of service * Manager approval required for certifications and external training * Mandatory training applies to all employees regardless of tenure == Process == # Identify training needs during performance or development discussions # Select courses via the Learning Portal # Submit approval request (if required) # Complete training and provide feedback # Upload certification proof (if applicable) == Tracking & Reporting == * Employees can track progress via the Learning Dashboard * Managers receive quarterly reports on team participation * HR monitors completion rates and program impact == Success Metrics == * Training completion rate (>85%) * Certification attainment rate * Internal promotion rate * Employee satisfaction with learning opportunities == FAQ == '''Q: Can I take training outside my role?'''
Yes, cross-functional learning is encouraged with manager approval. '''Q: What happens if I fail a certification exam?'''
One retake is reimbursed upon manager approval. __NOTOC__

== Overview == The Job Rotation and Shadowing Program enables employees to gain hands-on experience in different roles, teams, or departments. The program is designed to broaden skill sets, enhance collaboration, and support career exploration within the organization. == Objectives == * Develop cross-functional knowledge * Support career path exploration * Improve organizational agility * Strengthen collaboration across teams == Program Options == === Job Rotation === A structured, temporary assignment in a different role or department. '''Key features:''' * Duration: 3 to 6 months * Defined goals and deliverables * Partial or full role transition * Formal evaluation at completion === Job Shadowing === A short-term observational experience where employees follow a colleague to learn about their role. '''Key features:''' * Duration: 1 day to 4 weeks * Focus on observation and learning * No formal deliverables required * Flexible scheduling == Eligibility == * Minimum 6 months in current role * Consistent performance rating * Manager approval required * Open to all departments == Application Process == # Explore available opportunities on the Internal Mobility Portal # Discuss interest with current manager # Submit application with learning objectives # Participate in selection/interview (if required) # Agree on rotation/shadowing plan == Roles & Responsibilities == '''Employee:''' * Define clear learning goals * Actively participate and engage * Provide feedback after completion '''Current Manager:''' * Support transition planning * Ensure workload coverage * Provide input on development goals '''Host Manager:''' * Assign meaningful tasks or exposure * Provide guidance and feedback * Evaluate participation ----- === Benefits === '''For Employees:''' * Exposure to new skills and career paths * Expanded professional network * Increased job satisfaction '''For the Organization:''' * Improved talent mobility * Knowledge sharing across teams * Stronger internal talent pipeline == Evaluation & Feedback == * Mid-point check-in (for rotations) * Final feedback session with host manager * Post-program survey * Optional presentation of learnings == Example Use Cases == * HR specialist rotating into Talent Acquisition * Finance analyst shadowing a Business Partner * IT employee exploring a Product Management role == FAQ == '''Q: Will my salary or role change during rotation?'''
No, compensation remains unchanged during the program. '''Q: Can I apply for a permanent role after rotation?'''
Yes, participation can support internal mobility applications. __NOTOC__

At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" == Overview == This page provides a framework for how the Mentorship Program operates. It ensures that both mentors and mentees understand their roles, responsibilities, and expectations for a successful mentorship relationship. == Program Principles == * '''Mutual Respect''' – Mentors and mentees should maintain a professional, respectful, and supportive relationship. * '''Confidentiality''' – Discussions during mentorship sessions are confidential unless otherwise agreed. * '''Ownership''' – Mentees take responsibility for their learning; mentors provide guidance, not directives. * '''Commitment''' – Regular meetings and active participation are essential for a meaningful mentorship experience. == Roles and Responsibilities == === Mentor === * Provide constructive feedback and guidance. * Share knowledge, experiences, and practical advice. * Encourage independent thinking and self-reflection. === Mentee === * Set personal development goals and communicate progress. * Actively participate in mentorship sessions and practice new skills. * Seek feedback and be open to learning opportunities. === HR Business Partner === * Facilitate mentor-mentee pairing and track program participation. * Provide resources, templates, and guidance. * Collect feedback to improve the program. {{PanelStart |panelIcon = :yellow_star: |panelIconId = atlassian-yellow_star |panelIconText = :yellow_star: |bgColor = #FFFAE6 }} == Recommended Practices == * Schedule meetings in advance and prepare an agenda. * Agree on measurable goals and expected outcomes at the start. * Keep a brief log of discussions or action items (optional). * Review progress mid-cycle and adjust goals as needed. {{PanelEnd}} __NOTOC__

__TOC__ == Overview == Submitting an IT ticket ensures your issue is tracked, prioritized, and resolved efficiently. This article explains how to create a ticket using the company’s helpdesk portal, including what information to include and how to check the status of your request. == Before you begin == Before submitting a ticket, make sure you have: * A clear description of the problem * Any error messages or screenshots (optional but helpful) * The name of the device or application involved * The time the issue started Providing accurate details helps IT resolve your issue faster. == Submitting a ticket through the helpdesk portal == === 1. Go to the helpdesk portal === Open your browser and navigate to:
https://support.<yourcompany>.com === 2. Sign in === Use your company email and password to sign in to the portal. === 3. Select “Create ticket” or “New request” === This option is located on the main dashboard or in the top navigation bar. === 4. Choose the issue category === Select the category that best matches your problem, such as: * Software issue * Hardware issue * Account or password problem * Network or VPN * Printer or scanner * General request Choosing the correct category ensures the ticket is routed to the right team. === 5. Fill out the ticket form === Include the following details: * '''Subject:''' A short summary of your issue * '''Description:''' A clear explanation of the problem * '''Device or system affected:''' Laptop, VPN, email, printer, etc. * '''Urgency level:''' Standard, high, or critical * '''Attachments:''' Screenshots or error messages (recommended) === 6. Submit the ticket === Click '''Submit''', '''Send''', or '''Create''' depending on the portal’s wording.
You will receive a confirmation email with your ticket number. ----- == Submitting a ticket by email == If you cannot access the helpdesk portal, you can email your request to:
[mailto:support@yourcompany.com '''support@yourcompany.com'''] Include: * A clear subject line (e.g., “Cannot connect to Wi‑Fi”) * A detailed description of the issue * Screenshots or logs if available IT will create the ticket for you. ----- == Checking ticket status == You can view your ticket progress by logging into the helpdesk portal: # Go to '''My tickets''' or '''Requests'''. # Review the status (Open, In progress, Waiting on user, Resolved). # Add comments or upload additional information if requested. You will receive email updates whenever IT changes the ticket status or needs more information. ----- == Tips for faster resolution == * Provide as much detail as possible in your first message. * Respond quickly if IT requests additional information. * Attach screenshots, which often help diagnose issues faster. * For urgent issues (system outage, access emergency), mark the ticket as '''High priority'''.

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 24 April 2023 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 28 August 2023 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="dd7e91c6-3e1a-4664-816e-913d4d379afd" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="2b95241e-3c4a-4e97-85a1-52e525a82d59" ! ac:local-id="e3b23944-c51c-4935-873d-5ca0f7c07b73"| '''Action''' ! Employee Engagement Survey | ac:local-id="5308f2a862f3"| AS | ac:local-id="50acc1e692de"| 10 Apr | ac:local-id="3e2723fd8a4a"| In Progress |- ac:local-id="b0f89a6fe194" | ac:local-id="349af9354a7c"| Draft concept and agenda for first Quarterly Learning Day | ac:local-id="a96b63fd4c90"| DW | ac:local-id="51682a799aae"| 31 Mar | ac:local-id="f78ee64367db"| Not Started |- ac:local-id="379a4c665303" | ac:local-id="0aef6b74e314"| Review onboarding request process with IT to reduce tool access delays | ac:local-id="ab2e5dcca0fe"| LF | ac:local-id="f737ac88f437"| 15 Apr | ac:local-id="6bc7f64758a5"| Planned |- ac:local-id="b4c071dcea33" | ac:local-id="73a9ee9c0a87"| Research potential onboarding success metrics and reporting approach | ac:local-id="05ad1d9b1730"| DW | ac:local-id="c386305d9619"| 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

== Overview == Averion Solutions recognizes that '''strong social connections and a sense of community at work are essential for employee wellbeing, engagement, and collaboration'''. The Social & Team Wellbeing program is designed to foster '''positive relationships, team cohesion, and a supportive workplace culture''' through social initiatives, team activities, and recognition programs. This program encourages employees to '''connect, celebrate achievements, and build meaningful networks''', whether working on-site, hybrid, or remotely. == Program Components == === Team-Building Activities === * Regular team-building exercises, workshops, and offsites to strengthen collaboration. * Virtual and in-person activities such as problem-solving challenges, creative sessions, and trivia games. * Rotating activities to engage different teams and interests. === Social Events === * Company-wide events for celebrations, recognition, and networking. * Seasonal gatherings, wellness days, and informal “coffee breaks” or virtual social hours. * Opportunities for cross-departmental interaction to build connections. === Recognition & Appreciation Programs === * Peer-to-peer recognition initiatives to highlight contributions and achievements. * Monthly or quarterly awards for teamwork, innovation, and leadership. * Encouragement of small gestures of appreciation within teams. === Volunteer & Community Engagement === * Company-supported volunteer programs to give back to local communities. * Team participation in charity events, fundraisers, or social impact initiatives. * Recognition of employees contributing to social causes. === Supportive Culture Practices === * Encouraging open communication, active listening, and empathy in daily interactions. * Team rituals to celebrate milestones, birthdays, and achievements. * Inclusion of social wellbeing topics in onboarding and regular training sessions. == Employee Resources == * '''Event Calendar:''' Schedule of social activities, team-building events, and recognition programs. * '''Recognition Portal:''' Platform to submit peer recognition and view award announcements. * '''Volunteer Opportunities:''' List of upcoming company-supported community initiatives. * '''Guidelines & Tips:''' Best practices for fostering team cohesion and inclusive social interactions. {{Tip |body = == Participation Tips == * Join at least one social or team-building activity each month. * Celebrate colleagues’ contributions and milestones proactively. * Participate in volunteer or community engagement initiatives. * Provide feedback and ideas to continuously improve team wellbeing programs. }} __NOTOC__

Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default"
