managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

__TOC__ == Overview == This article explains how employees can request access to company applications, shared folders, network drives, and other protected resources. Following the correct process ensures access is properly reviewed, approved, and granted according to security and compliance requirements. ----- == Types of access you can request == Employees may request access to: * Business applications (CRM, ERP, HR systems, development tools, etc.) * Shared network drives or folders * SharePoint sites and Teams channels * Internal web portals or databases * Specialized tools for project or department use * Distribution lists or shared mailboxes Some access types require additional approvals or training. ----- == Submitting an access request == === 1. Go to the helpdesk portal === Navigate to:
https://support.<yourcompany>.com === 2. Sign in === Use your company email and password. === 3. Select the appropriate request type === Look for one of the following categories: * '''Application access request''' * '''Shared folder or network drive access''' * '''SharePoint/Teams access''' * '''Mailbox or distribution list access''' * '''Database access''' Choose the type that best matches the access you need. === 4. Complete the request form === Provide the details required for processing: * '''Name of the resource or application''' * '''Type of access needed''' (read‑only, edit, admin, etc.) * '''Reason for access''' (project, job role, temporary need) * '''Your department or team''' * '''Manager approval''' (required for most access types) * '''Usernames or accounts to be added''' (if different from your own) Adding clear detail helps avoid delays. === 5. Submit the request === After submission, you will receive a confirmation email with the request number. ----- == Approval process == === Manager approval === Most access requests require manager approval to ensure the access is appropriate for your role. === Application or resource owner approval === For high‑risk or specialized systems, the resource owner or data owner must approve the request.
Examples include: * Finance or HR systems * Production databases * Sensitive shared folders * Admin‑level system access === IT review === The IT team will: * Validate the request * Confirm required approvals * Assign the correct permissions * Ensure access aligns with security policies ----- == Access fulfillment == Once approved: * Application access will be assigned to your account * Shared drives or folders will appear automatically after you log in again * SharePoint and Teams access will become available immediately or after a refresh * Mailbox or distribution list access may require restarting your mail program You will receive a confirmation email when the access has been granted. ----- == Requesting changes or removing access == If you need changes to your access: * Submit an '''access modification request''' through the helpdesk portal. * Specify what needs to be added, changed, or removed. If you no longer need access, submit an '''access removal''' request.
Removing unnecessary access helps maintain security and compliance. ----- == Troubleshooting == === You cannot see a shared folder === Try: * Signing out and back into your device * Restarting your computer * Reconnecting to the company VPN (if remote) If still missing, the permissions may not have been applied yet. === You receive “access denied” errors === This usually means: * Permissions have not been fully applied * Your request needs additional approval * You were assigned the wrong access level Submit a follow‑up via your ticket if this happens. === SharePoint or Teams access not showing === Try: * Refreshing the site or app * Signing out and back in * Clearing your browser cache Changes may take a few minutes to sync across apps.

At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" == '''Overview''' == At Averion Solutions, we believe that recognizing and celebrating employee contributions strengthens engagement, reinforces company values, and fosters a positive workplace culture. The '''Recognition & Engagement''' programs are designed to acknowledge achievements, encourage collaboration, and reward innovative thinking. These initiatives provide structured recognition opportunities, peer-to-peer acknowledgment, and award programs that motivate employees to excel and feel valued. == '''Recognition & Engagement Pillars''' == * '''Employee Recognition Programs''' – Formal awards and peer-nominated recognitions. * '''Team Engagement Activities''' – Social events, team-building exercises, and collaborative challenges. * '''Innovation & Suggestion Programs''' – Opportunities for employees to propose improvements or new ideas. * '''Spotlight & Shout-Out Initiatives''' – Quick, informal recognition for day-to-day contributions. == '''Programs''' == {{SubpageList|depth=2|allChildren=true|style=|sortAndReverse=|first=0|page=}} == '''How to Participate / Access Programs''' == * All programs are available to Averion Solutions employees. * Participation can be initiated by managers, peers, or employees themselves depending on the program. * For guidance on submitting nominations, registering for events, or sharing ideas, visit the individual program pages or contact the '''Employee Engagement Coordinator'''. __NOTOC__

5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 18 August 2025 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" == Overview == The Offer Process begins once a candidate has been selected and the hiring decision has been finalized. It ensures that compensation, benefits, and start dates are aligned with company policies and internal approvals, and that the candidate receives a clear and professional offer. This stage transitions candidates from “selected” to “incoming employee,” setting the stage for onboarding. == Objectives == * Confirm offer details are accurate and compliant * Communicate the offer effectively to the candidate * Prepare all pre-onboarding tasks for a smooth first day == Process Overview == The Offer Process consists of three main stages: # '''Offer Approval''' – internal review and sign-off # '''Offer Communication''' – presenting the offer to the candidate # '''Pre-Onboarding Preparation''' – completing administrative and logistical tasks Each stage has associated roles and responsibilities to ensure clarity and accountability. == Roles & Responsibilities == '''HR / Talent Acquisition:''' * Prepare the offer package * Ensure compliance with policies * Coordinate approvals * Communicate the offer '''Hiring Manager:''' * Validate proposed compensation and benefits * Support final approval if required * Join offer discussions when necessary '''Finance / Leadership:''' * Review budget alignment * Approve for senior or strategic roles == Notes == * Some offers may require executive or legal review * Time-sensitive roles may use an expedited offer process * Pre-onboarding tasks may start before formal offer acceptance in certain cases __NOTOC__

The team interview helps assess how well the candidate will integrate into the existing team. == '''Objectives''' == * Evaluate collaboration and communication style * Assess cultural and interpersonal fit * Provide candidates with insight into team dynamics == '''Format''' == * Panel interview or informal discussion * May include practical or scenario-based questions == '''Good Practices''' == * Encourage open and honest dialogue * Allow time for candidate questions * Avoid overly repetitive or unstructured discussions __NOTOC__

hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

Depending on the role, candidates may complete a structured assessment to evaluate practical skills. == '''Formats may include''' == * Take-home assignment * Live coding or problem-solving session * Case study discussion * Portfolio review * Presentation == '''Guidelines''' == * Ensure tasks are relevant to real job scenarios * Keep time expectations reasonable (generally < 4 hours for take-home tasks) * Provide clear instructions and evaluation criteria * Avoid over-engineered or academic exercises '''Candidate Experience Tip'''
Candidates should always be informed in advance about: * Expected duration * Evaluation criteria * Submission format __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 10 February 2025 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

== Overview == The Cultural Awareness Workshops are interactive learning sessions designed to increase understanding, respect, and appreciation of diverse cultures, perspectives, and experiences within the workplace. These workshops support the development of an inclusive and globally aware organization. == Objectives == * Build cultural competence across teams * Reduce unconscious bias and stereotypes * Improve cross-cultural communication * Foster an inclusive and respectful work environment == Workshop Topics == * Unconscious Bias Awareness * Cross-Cultural Communication * Inclusive Language and Behavior * Global Working Styles * Accessibility and Inclusion == Format == * Instructor-led sessions (virtual or in-person) * Interactive discussions and case studies * Group exercises and scenario-based learning * Optional follow-up resources and materials '''Duration:''' * Typically 60–120 minutes per session == Participation == * Open to all employees * Some sessions may be mandatory (e.g., for managers or hiring teams) * Advanced workshops available for leadership roles == Facilitators == * Internal HR / DEI specialists * External experts and guest speakers * Certified trainers for specialized topics == Learning Outcomes == Participants will: * Recognize and address unconscious biases * Communicate more effectively across cultures * Apply inclusive behaviors in daily work * Contribute to a more respectful workplace == Feedback & Evaluation == * Post-session surveys * Knowledge checks or reflection exercises * Ongoing feedback channels == Success Metrics == * Participation rates * Employee feedback scores * Improvement in inclusion-related survey results * Behavioral changes observed by managers == Example Workshop Journey == '''Step 1:''' Introductory session (Unconscious Bias)
'''Step 2:''' Core workshop (Cross-Cultural Communication)
'''Step 3:''' Advanced topic (Inclusive Leadership)
'''Step 4:''' Follow-up resources and reinforcement == FAQ == '''Q: Are these workshops mandatory?'''
Some foundational sessions may be required depending on role. '''Q: Can teams request custom sessions?'''
Yes, tailored workshops can be arranged through HR. __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 03 February 2025 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" The interview process at Averion Solutions is designed to assess both the '''technical capabilities''' and '''cultural alignment''' of candidates in a '''structured and consistent way'''. A well-defined interview framework ensures that all candidates are evaluated fairly while providing a positive and transparent experience. Interviews are conducted collaboratively and typically involve HR, the hiring manager, and selected team members. Each stage of the interview process focuses on different aspects of candidate evaluation, from general fit to role-specific expertise. While the structure below represents the standard approach, the exact number of interview stages may vary depending on the role, seniority level, and hiring urgency. == Interview Stages == A typical interview process includes the following stages: # [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Interview_Process/HR_Interview|HR Interview]] # [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Interview_Process/Hiring_Manager_Interview|Hiring Manager Interview]] # [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Interview_Process/Technical_Assessment|Technical / Skills Assessment]] # [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Interview_Process/Team_Interview|Team Interview]] '''Not all roles require all stages'''. For example, junior roles may skip technical assessments, while senior roles may include additional stakeholder interviews. == Interview Scorecards == Each interviewer should use the provided [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Interview_Process/Interview_Scorecard_Template|template]]to take notes during an interview. The collected information should be the basis for internal disucssions regarding a candidate’s fit for the position/company. __NOTOC__

== Overview == Sourcing and screening are critical stages in the hiring process, focused on identifying qualified candidates and narrowing the applicant pool to those who best match the role requirements. At Averion Solutions, sourcing is primarily managed by HR / Talent Acquisition, while screening is conducted in close collaboration with hiring managers where needed. The effectiveness of these stages has a direct impact on both hiring speed and candidate quality. == Objectives == * Attract a diverse and relevant candidate pool * Ensure fair and consistent evaluation of applicants * Reduce time-to-hire through efficient screening * Maintain a positive candidate experience == Process Overview == This stage consists of two main components: === Sourcing === Identifying and attracting potential candidates through various channels. '''See''': [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Sourcing_and_Screening/Sourcing_Channels|Sourcing Channels]] === Screening === Evaluating incoming applications and shortlisting candidates for interviews. '''See:'''[[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Sourcing_and_Screening/Screening_Guidelines|Screening Guidelines]] == Roles & Responsibilities == '''HR / Talent Acquisition''' * Publish job postings * Manage sourcing channels * Conduct initial screening * Coordinate candidate communication '''Hiring Manager''' * Provide input on candidate profiles * Support screening decisions (if required) * Review shortlisted candidates == Key Outputs == * Active candidate pipeline * Shortlist of qualified candidates * Initial screening feedback == Common Challenges == '''Low application volume'''
→ May require expanding sourcing channels or adjusting job description '''High volume of unqualified candidates'''
→ Often caused by unclear or overly broad job descriptions '''Screening inconsistencies'''
→ Can lead to bias or missed strong candidates == Notes == * Some roles (especially technical or niche positions) may require proactive sourcing in addition to job postings * Screening depth may vary depending on role seniority and urgency __NOTOC__

== Overview == Averion Solutions uses a mix of internal and external sourcing channels to attract candidates. The choice of channels depends on the role, seniority level, and hiring urgency. Not all channels are used consistently across departments, and effectiveness may vary over time. == Primary Channels == === Company Careers Page === All open roles are published on the company careers page. '''Notes:''' * Serves as the central source of truth for open positions * Typically integrated with the applicant tracking system === Professional Networking Platforms === Commonly used platforms include: * LinkedIn * Xing (primarily for DACH region) '''Use cases:''' * Active sourcing (outreach to candidates) * Job postings * Employer branding === Job Boards === Roles may be posted on general or specialized job boards. '''Examples:''' * General job boards * Industry-specific platforms (depending on role) '''Note:'''
Effectiveness can vary significantly depending on the role. === Employee Referral Program === Employee referrals are encouraged and often result in strong candidates. '''Benefits:''' * Faster hiring process * Better cultural alignment * Higher retention (observed, but not formally tracked) '''Note:'''
Referral bonuses may apply depending on role and region. === Recruitment Agencies === External agencies may be used for: * Hard-to-fill roles * Senior or specialized positions '''Considerations:''' * Additional cost * Requires clear briefing and alignment == Additional / Occasionally Used Channels == * University partnerships * Internal talent pools * Networking events or meetups * Previous applicants == Channel Selection Guidelines == When selecting sourcing channels, consider: * Role seniority * Market availability of talent * Urgency of hire * Budget constraints == Common Challenges == '''Over-reliance on one channel'''
→ Can limit candidate diversity '''Outdated job postings'''
→ Leads to poor candidate experience '''Untracked sourcing effectiveness'''
→ Makes it difficult to optimize strategy == Notes == * Channel performance is not consistently tracked across all roles * Some teams maintain their own preferred sourcing channels * Best-performing channels may vary by region __NOTOC__

== '''Overview''' == At Averion Solutions, supporting employees’ mental and emotional wellbeing is a top priority. The '''Employee Assistance Program (EAP)''' provides confidential, professional support to help employees manage personal or work-related challenges, ensuring they can maintain balance, resilience, and productivity. The EAP is available to all employees and covers a wide range of issues, from stress management to family concerns, legal or financial guidance, and career support. == '''Services Offered''' == The EAP provides '''confidential and professional assistance''' in the following areas: * '''Mental Health & Counseling''' ** One-on-one counseling sessions (in-person or virtual) ** Stress, anxiety, or depression management ** Crisis support and intervention * '''Family & Relationship Support''' ** Guidance on parenting, childcare, and family dynamics ** Relationship counseling * '''Work-Related Support''' ** Managing workplace stress, conflict resolution, and performance challenges ** Career coaching and professional development guidance * '''Financial & Legal Guidance''' ** Budgeting, debt management, retirement planning ** Access to legal consultations and document review * '''Wellbeing Resources''' ** Online workshops, webinars, and articles on health, mindfulness, and personal growth ** Access to meditation apps, self-assessment tools, and resilience programs == '''How to Access the EAP''' == * '''Confidentiality Guaranteed:''' All interactions with the EAP are private. Personal information is not shared with managers or HR without consent. * '''Contact Methods:''' ** '''Phone:''' [EAP hotline number] ** '''Email:''' [EAP email address] ** '''Online Portal:''' [Link to EAP portal] * '''Availability:''' Services are available '''24/7''' for urgent matters, with scheduled sessions for non-emergency support. == '''Participation Guidelines''' == * EAP services are '''voluntary and free''' for all employees. * Employees may attend as many sessions as needed within program limits. * Managers may refer employees, but participation is '''always optional'''. === '''Resources & Links''' === * '''Self-help articles and videos''' * '''Meditation & mindfulness app''' * '''Stress management workshop registration''' __NOTOC__

The HR interview serves as the initial touchpoint between the candidate and Averion Solutions. == '''Objectives:''' == * Validate candidate background and experience * Understand motivation and career goals * Assess basic cultural alignment * Clarify logistics (availability, location, compensation expectations) == '''Typical Topics:''' == * Career progression and key achievements * Reasons for applying * Expectations toward role and company * Work preferences (remote, hybrid, office) == '''Guidelines for HR:''' == * Keep the conversation structured but conversational * Avoid making early judgments based on limited information * Ensure consistent questions across candidates for the same role __NOTOC__

Averion Solutions regularly evaluates recruitment processes to improve efficiency, fairness, and candidate experience.
Feedback is collected from:
* Hiring managers * Interview panel members * New employees * Candidates (when possible)
Insights from this feedback help refine interview practices, improve job descriptions, and streamline recruitment workflows. __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 17 February 2025 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" Employee Engagement Survey | ac:local-id="5308f2a862f3"| AS | ac:local-id="50acc1e692de"| 10 Apr | ac:local-id="3e2723fd8a4a"| In Progress |- ac:local-id="b0f89a6fe194" | ac:local-id="349af9354a7c"| Draft concept and agenda for first Quarterly Learning Day | ac:local-id="a96b63fd4c90"| DW | ac:local-id="51682a799aae"| 31 Mar | ac:local-id="f78ee64367db"| Not Started |- ac:local-id="379a4c665303" | ac:local-id="0aef6b74e314"| Review onboarding request process with IT to reduce tool access delays | ac:local-id="ab2e5dcca0fe"| LF | ac:local-id="f737ac88f437"| 15 Apr | ac:local-id="6bc7f64758a5"| Planned |- ac:local-id="b4c071dcea33" | ac:local-id="73a9ee9c0a87"| Research potential onboarding success metrics and reporting approach | ac:local-id="05ad1d9b1730"| DW | ac:local-id="c386305d9619"| 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

== Overview == Pre-onboarding ensures that all administrative, technical, and logistical preparations are complete before the new employee’s first day. A smooth pre-onboarding experience helps candidates feel welcomed and ready to contribute immediately. == Typical Tasks == '''HR / Operations''' * Prepare employment contract and confirm signed documents * Register employee in HR and payroll systems * Set up benefits enrollment '''IT Support''' * Request and prepare laptop, phone, and other equipment * Provision email and collaboration tool accounts * Configure access permissions and security credentials '''Hiring Manager / Team''' * Assign onboarding buddy * Schedule introductory meetings and training sessions * Prepare first-week plan and role overview == Timeline == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="b55770da-5409-413a-8351-432c41c70a24" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="d7552021-dad8-4ab8-be5b-ea61b58db58e" ! ac:local-id="a0ecd640-6909-419b-8dbd-fd50fea6c9ce"| '''Task''' ! ac:local-id="797eb7fc-824a-4d1b-a464-4df60168ecf9"| '''Responsible''' ! ac:local-id="f05808ef-aaa2-449b-8cb6-1919b7454a7a"| '''Deadline''' |- ac:local-id="5413e698-37b1-4749-b2bc-13c6da7e15bd" | ac:local-id="d117e8e2-eec6-4a4c-98b4-0948e97f506a"| Contract preparation | ac:local-id="24371a41-5f62-455f-9ecb-874322f3742f"| HR | ac:local-id="d5e3e28a-929d-4f35-92dd-20e0c481f63f"| 1 week before start |- ac:local-id="5c3d54c5-e372-4cdb-addb-71f84732d083" | ac:local-id="a8f2b46d-3710-490c-bb6a-1ae5c4632696"| IT equipment ready | ac:local-id="8dffccf1-1769-4db0-9983-fc3cfa3caa24"| IT | ac:local-id="1d33b33e-3b1a-442e-b385-7f464a5d054e"| 2–3 days before start |- ac:local-id="b4452ded-5158-4690-a26b-d860244d98c2" | ac:local-id="3cd7c5e6-54c5-4964-b863-a8d93260ab57"| Access accounts provisioned | ac:local-id="8af08808-04da-436b-b6a1-780bc71fe80a"| IT | ac:local-id="ab7130ef-ad65-4ee0-9e80-220d03a362e8"| 2–3 days before start |- ac:local-id="e5ddaa43-da77-431e-8b09-7f196a9c0623" | ac:local-id="bf0d0ef1-efb0-43d3-89ac-a8026aae205f"| First-week schedule | ac:local-id="d871a313-989a-4afb-a0c9-16cbb4ac78fb"| Hiring Manager | ac:local-id="c251dd1c-6602-425e-a0c8-a8b8cc8d7714"| 1 week before start |} == Notes == * Some pre-onboarding activities may begin before formal offer acceptance for urgent roles * Communication with the new hire should confirm logistics, expectations, and welcome materials * HR may provide a pre-onboarding packet with policies, guidelines, and team introduction == See also == [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Onboarding_Process|Onboarding Process]] __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 10 March 2025 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default"
