The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 18 March 2024 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" == '''Overview''' == At Averion Solutions, we are committed to fostering a workplace where all employees feel valued, respected, and empowered to succeed. The '''Diversity, Equity & Inclusion (DEI)''' programs are designed to create an inclusive culture, celebrate differences, and ensure equal opportunities for growth and advancement. These initiatives provide education, resources, and actionable programs to promote fairness, understanding, and collaboration across all teams. == '''DEI Pillars''' == * '''Inclusive Hiring & Career Growth''' – Ensuring equitable recruitment, promotion, and development practices. * '''Education & Awareness Programs''' – Training, workshops, and events to build cultural competency and reduce bias. * '''Employee Resource Groups (ERGs)''' – Voluntary, employee-led groups that provide community, support, and advocacy. * '''Inclusive Culture Initiatives''' – Programs and activities that encourage diverse perspectives, collaboration, and belonging. == '''Programs''' == {{SubpageList|depth=2|allChildren=true|style=|sortAndReverse=|first=0|page=}} == '''How to Participate / Access Programs''' == * All programs are available to Averion Solutions employees. * Participation is voluntary. Employees are encouraged to join ERGs, attend trainings, and engage with initiatives aligned with their interests. * For guidance on participating or learning more, visit the individual program pages or contact the '''DEI Program Coordinator'''. __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 14 July 2025 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" Employee Engagement Survey | ac:local-id="5308f2a862f3"| AS | ac:local-id="50acc1e692de"| 10 Apr | ac:local-id="3e2723fd8a4a"| In Progress |- ac:local-id="b0f89a6fe194" | ac:local-id="349af9354a7c"| Draft concept and agenda for first Quarterly Learning Day | ac:local-id="a96b63fd4c90"| DW | ac:local-id="51682a799aae"| 31 Mar | ac:local-id="f78ee64367db"| Not Started |- ac:local-id="379a4c665303" | ac:local-id="0aef6b74e314"| Review onboarding request process with IT to reduce tool access delays | ac:local-id="ab2e5dcca0fe"| LF | ac:local-id="f737ac88f437"| 15 Apr | ac:local-id="6bc7f64758a5"| Planned |- ac:local-id="b4c071dcea33" | ac:local-id="73a9ee9c0a87"| Research potential onboarding success metrics and reporting approach | ac:local-id="05ad1d9b1730"| DW | ac:local-id="c386305d9619"| 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

== '''Overview''' == At Averion Solutions, we are committed to fostering continuous learning, skill growth, and career progression. The '''Career Development & Learning''' programs are designed to help employees expand capabilities, explore new opportunities, and achieve their professional goals while contributing to the success of their teams and the organization. These initiatives provide structured programs, mentoring, training, and exposure opportunities to support professional growth across all levels. == '''Career Development Pillars''' == * '''Mentorship & Coaching''' – Structured mentor–mentee relationships and coaching support. * '''Training & Certification Programs''' – Access to internal and external courses to develop key skills. * '''Job Rotation & Shadowing Opportunities''' – Hands-on experience in new roles or departments. * '''Career Planning & Development Tools''' – Resources for goal-setting, personal development, and career mapping. == '''Programs''' == {{SubpageList|depth=2|allChildren=true|style=|sortAndReverse=|first=0|page=}} == '''How to Participate / Access Programs''' == * All programs are available to Averion Solutions employees. * Participation is voluntary, and many programs can be accessed online or in person. * For guidance on registration or participation, visit the individual program pages or contact the '''Learning & Development Coordinator'''. __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 12 May 2025 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" {| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 27 November 2023 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new {| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 30 September 2024 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 13 October 2025 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR {| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 11 September 2023 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__
