== Overview == The hiring workflow at Averion Solutions provides a structured, end-to-end view of the recruitment process, from initial role identification to candidate onboarding. It is designed to ensure alignment between business needs, hiring decisions, and candidate experience. This page serves as a high-level guide to the hiring process. Each stage is supported by more detailed documentation, templates, and guidelines available in the corresponding subpages. While the workflow below reflects the standard process, certain roles or departments may follow slightly modified steps depending on urgency, seniority, or regional requirements. == End-to-End Workflow == The hiring process consists of the following key stages: * [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Workforce_Planning_and_Role_Approval|Workforce Planning & Role Approval]] * [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Job_Description_Creation|Job Description & Role Definition]] * [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Sourcing_and_Screening|Sourcing & Job Posting]] * [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Sourcing_and_Screening/Screening_Guidelines|Candidate Screening]] * [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Interview_Process|Interview Process]] * [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Candidate_Evaluation_and_Decision|Candidate Evaluation & Decision]] * [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Offer_Process|Offer Preparation & Approval]] * [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Offer_Process/Pre-Onboarding_Checklist|Offer Acceptance & Pre-Onboarding]] Each stage is outlined below with key activities, owners, and typical outputs. == Workforce Planning & Role Approval == '''Objective:'''
Identify and validate the need for a new role. '''Key Activities:''' * Assess team capacity and workload * Define business justification * Confirm budget availability * Submit and approve hiring request '''Primary Owners:'''
Hiring Manager, HR Business Partner, Finance, Department Director '''Output:'''
Approved hiring request '''See''': [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Workforce_Planning_and_Role_Approval|Workforce Planning & Role Approval]] == Job Description & Role Definition == '''Objective:'''
Clearly define the role requirements and expectations. '''Key Activities:''' * Draft job description * Define responsibilities and success criteria * Align on required qualifications and experience * Review for consistency and compliance '''Primary Owners:'''
Hiring Manager, HR Business Partner '''Output:'''
Approved job description '''See''': [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Job_Description_Creation|Job Description Creation]] == Sourcing & Job Posting == '''Objective:'''
Attract a qualified and diverse pool of candidates. '''Key Activities:''' * Publish job posting across channels * Activate employee referral program * Engage external partners (if required) * Monitor application pipeline '''Primary Owners:'''
HR / Talent Acquisition '''Output:'''
Active candidate pipeline '''See:''' [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Sourcing_and_Screening|Sourcing & Screening]] == Candidate Screening == '''Objective:'''
Shortlist candidates who meet the core role requirements. '''Key Activities:''' * Review applications and resumes * Conduct initial screening calls (if needed) * Evaluate baseline qualifications * Create shortlist for interviews '''Primary Owners:'''
HR / Talent Acquisition '''Output:'''
Shortlisted candidates '''See:''' [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Sourcing_and_Screening/Screening_Guidelines|Screening Guidelines]] == Interview Process == '''Objective:'''
Assess candidate suitability through structured evaluation. '''Key Activities:''' * Conduct HR interview * Conduct hiring manager interview * Perform technical or skills assessments * Facilitate team interviews '''Primary Owners:'''
HR, Hiring Manager, Interview Panel '''Output:'''
Completed interview feedback '''See:''' [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Interview_Process|Interview Process]] == Candidate Evaluation & Decision == '''Objective:'''
Select the most suitable candidate based on structured feedback. '''Key Activities:''' * Collect and review interview feedback * Compare candidates using defined criteria * Conduct alignment discussions (if needed) * Make final hiring decision '''Primary Owners:'''
Hiring Manager, HR '''Output:'''
Selected candidate '''See:''' [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Candidate_Evaluation_and_Decision|Candidate Evaluation & Decision]] == Offer Preparation & Approval == '''Objective:'''
Prepare a competitive and internally aligned offer. '''Key Activities:''' * Define compensation package * Validate against salary bands and budget * Obtain internal approvals * Prepare employment contract '''Primary Owners:'''
HR, Finance, Hiring Manager '''Output:'''
Approved offer '''See:''' [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Offer_Process|Offer Process]] == Offer Acceptance & Pre-Onboarding == '''Objective:'''
Ensure a smooth transition from candidate to employee. '''Key Activities:''' * Conduct offer call and confirm acceptance * Share contract and onboarding materials * Initiate IT and HR setup * Prepare first-day experience '''Primary Owners:'''
HR, Hiring Manager, IT '''Output:'''
Confirmed hire and onboarding readiness '''See:''' [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Offer_Process/Pre-Onboarding_Checklist|Pre-Onboarding Checklist]] == Typical Hiring Timeline == While timelines may vary, the standard hiring process typically takes '''3–6 weeks''' from role approval to offer acceptance. '''Indicative timeline:''' * '''Week 1–2:''' Role approval and job posting * '''Week 2–4:''' Candidate sourcing and interviews * '''Week 4–5:''' Final interviews and decision * '''Week 5–6:''' Offer preparation and acceptance Delays may occur due to candidate availability, internal alignment, or role complexity. == Roles Involved in the Hiring Workflow == The hiring process is a cross-functional effort involving multiple stakeholders: * '''Hiring Manager''' – Defines role requirements and makes final decision * '''HR Business Partner''' – Ensures process compliance and supports decision-making * '''HR Operations''' – Handles administrative and onboarding tasks * '''Interview Panel''' – Provides structured candidate feedback * '''Finance''' – Validates budget and compensation * '''IT Support''' – Prepares technical setup for new hires '''See:''' [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Roles_and_Responsibilies|Roles & Responsibilities]] == Process Variations == While this workflow represents the standard approach, some variations may apply: * '''Senior roles:''' Additional interview stages or executive alignment * '''Urgent hires:''' Accelerated process with fewer steps * '''Regional roles:''' Local compliance or process adaptations == Known Gaps / Ongoing Improvements == * In some cases, interview feedback is not submitted in a timely manner * Coordination between interviewers can be inconsistent for high-volume roles * Offer approval timelines may vary depending on department These areas are currently being reviewed as part of ongoing process improvements. __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 11 December 2023 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

== Overview == The role approval process ensures that all new hiring requests are reviewed, validated, and approved before recruitment begins. This process helps maintain alignment between hiring plans, budget constraints, and organizational priorities. == Process Steps == === Identify Hiring Need === The hiring manager identifies a need based on: * Team capacity * Project requirements * Strategic initiatives At this stage, the need should be clearly articulated but does not need to be fully documented. === Define Role Scope === The hiring manager works with HR to: * Define responsibilities * Determine required experience level * Clarify reporting structure * Align on employment type A draft job description is typically created at this stage. === Validate Budget === Before submitting a request, budget availability must be confirmed. '''Handled by:''' * Finance Business Partner * Department leadership This includes: * Headcount validation * Salary range alignment === Submit Hiring Request === The hiring manager submits the hiring request including: * Business justification * Role details * Budget confirmation '''See:''' [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Workforce_Planning_and_Role_Approval/Hiring_Request_Checklist|Hiring Request Checklist]] === HR Review === HR reviews the request to ensure: * Alignment with job frameworks * Consistency with similar roles * Compliance with internal policies HR may request adjustments before proceeding. === Leadership Approval === Final approval is provided by: * Department Director (or equivalent) This step confirms: * Strategic alignment * Priority relative to other hiring needs === Approval Confirmation === Once approved: * HR confirms the role can be opened * Recruitment process officially begins == Typical Timeline == * Standard approval: '''2–5 business days''' * Complex roles or new headcount: may take longer == Fast-Track Scenarios == In urgent cases: * Steps may be combined * Verbal approvals may be accepted temporarily '''Important:'''
Formal approval '''must still be documented afterward'''. == Common Bottlenecks == * Missing or unclear business justification * Budget not pre-aligned * Delayed leadership availability == Notes & Variations == * Some teams require additional regional or functional approvals * For senior roles, executive approval may be required __NOTOC__

DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 22 July 2024 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" == Overview == Screening is the process of evaluating incoming applications to identify candidates who meet the core requirements of a role and should proceed to the interview stage. At Averion Solutions, screening is typically conducted by HR, with input from hiring managers where necessary. == Objectives == * Identify candidates who meet baseline qualifications * Ensure fair and consistent evaluation * Create a manageable shortlist for interviews == Screening Process == === Application Review === Each application is reviewed based on: * Relevant experience * Skills and qualifications * Career progression * Alignment with role requirements === Initial Qualification Check === Key criteria include: * Must-have skills * Required years of experience * Location or work eligibility (if applicable) Candidates who do not meet minimum requirements are typically not progressed. === CV Evaluation === During CV review, consider: * Clarity and structure * Relevant achievements (not just responsibilities) * Consistency in career history '''Note:'''
Gaps or non-linear career paths should not automatically disqualify candidates. === Optional HR Screening Call === For selected candidates, a short screening call may be conducted. '''Purpose:''' * Clarify experience and expectations * Assess communication skills * Confirm logistics (availability, salary expectations) == Screening Criteria == Typical evaluation dimensions: * Technical or functional fit * Role relevance * Communication clarity * Potential for growth == Shortlisting == After screening, candidates are categorized: * '''Proceed to interview''' * '''Hold (potential backup candidates)''' * '''Reject''' == Documentation == Screening decisions should be documented briefly in the system, including: * Reason for rejection or progression * Key observations == Common Pitfalls == '''Over-filtering candidates'''
→ May result in missing strong but non-traditional profiles '''Inconsistent criteria'''
→ Leads to unfair comparisons '''Relying too heavily on CVs alone'''
→ May overlook potential == Tips for Effective Screening == * Focus on must-have criteria first * Avoid making assumptions based on limited information * When in doubt, consider progressing the candidate == Notes == * Screening depth may vary depending on application volume * For high-volume roles, screening may be more standardized * Some hiring managers prefer to review all CVs directly __NOTOC__

{{Info |body = Confluence does not automatically generate a TOC. }} Wikis are collaborative platforms designed to help teams create, share, and maintain knowledge efficiently. This page provides an overview of common wiki features and how they enhance documentation, teamwork, and information accessibility. {{PanelStart |panelIcon = :teal_star: |panelIconId = atlassian-teal_star |panelIconText = :teal_star: |bgColor = #E6FCFF }} Vertical TOC with heading levels limited to main level, with numbering: {{PanelEnd}} __TOC__ Wikis are collaborative platforms designed to help teams create, share, and maintain knowledge efficiently. This page provides an overview of common wiki features and how they enhance documentation, teamwork, and information accessibility. {{PanelStart |panelIcon = :teal_star: |panelIconId = atlassian-teal_star |panelIconText = :teal_star: |bgColor = #E6FCFF }} Horizontal TOC with heading levels limited to main level, no numbering: {{PanelEnd}} __TOC__ == Collaboration Features == === Real‑Time Editing === Modern wikis allow multiple users to edit a page at the same time. Edits appear instantly, reducing version conflicts and helping teams work together smoothly. === Commenting and Discussions === Inline comments and discussion threads let users give feedback, ask questions, and clarify content without modifying the main document. == Content Organization == === Hierarchical Page Structure === Wikis typically organize content in a tree-like structure. This makes it easy to group related pages and create intuitive navigation paths. === Labels and Tagging === Tags or labels help categorize content across spaces or topics, enabling users to filter and find pages quickly. == Search and Navigation == === Full‑Text Search === Wikis include powerful search engines that index page titles, body text, and attachments—ensuring users can locate information fast. === Navigation Panels and Breadcrumbs === Sidebars, page trees, and breadcrumbs help users understand where they are in the documentation and jump between related content easily. == Version Control == === Page History === Every change is stored as a version, allowing users to compare revisions, restore previous versions, and track contributions over time. === Change Notifications === Users can watch pages or entire spaces, receiving alerts whenever updates occur. == Content Enhancement Tools == === Templates === Page templates standardize content creation by providing predefined layouts for recurring documentation types—like meeting notes, project plans, or knowledge articles. == Access and Permissions == === User Roles and Permissions === Admins can control who can view, edit, or delete content. This ensures information is protected while still supporting collaboration.

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 04 August 2025 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

== Overview == At Averion Solutions, we understand that '''a healthy balance between professional and personal life is key to employee wellbeing, engagement, and productivity'''. The Work-Life Balance & Flexible Work program provides policies, resources, and tools that empower employees to '''manage their work schedules, responsibilities, and personal priorities effectively'''. This program supports a culture of '''trust, autonomy, and flexibility''', helping employees thrive both inside and outside the workplace. == Program Components == === Flexible Work Arrangements === * Options include '''remote, hybrid, and flexible hours''', depending on role and team needs. * Employees can adjust start and end times while ensuring collaboration and productivity. * Clear expectations are set for availability, communication, and performance outcomes. === Paid Time Off & Leave Programs === * PTO policies include vacation, personal days, wellness days, and parental leave. * Encouragement to use leave for rest, recovery, and personal commitments. * Special leave programs to support caregiving, family responsibilities, or personal milestones. === Wellness & Recharge Initiatives === * Company-sponsored mental health days or wellbeing leave. * Guidelines for taking regular breaks during the workday to prevent burnout. * Programs encouraging digital detox, reduced after-hours communications, and mindful scheduling. === Manager Support & Team Agreements === * Managers collaborate with team members to design schedules that balance business needs and personal priorities. * Team agreements outline core hours, meeting expectations, and remote work protocols. * Regular check-ins to review workload, stress levels, and flexibility requirements. === Resources & Training === * Workshops on time management, boundary-setting, and stress reduction. * Access to tools for planning and tracking work, personal projects, and leave. * Guidance for employees and managers on balancing flexibility with accountability. == Employee Resources == * '''Flexible Work Guidelines:''' Clear explanation of remote, hybrid, and flexible schedules. * '''PTO & Leave Portal:''' Centralized location to request and track time off. * '''Time Management Tips:''' Articles, templates, and webinars to improve work-life integration. * '''Manager Support Resources:''' Training for leaders to foster a culture of trust and flexibility. == Participation Tips == {{Tip |body = * Regularly review your workload and personal priorities. * Set clear boundaries between work and personal life, including start/end times. * Take advantage of flexible work options and wellness days to recharge. * Communicate openly with managers and teams about schedule needs. }} __NOTOC__

{{Info |body = [[#api|This link jumps to the anchor “api”]]. The paragraph is highlighted on the page. }} = Understanding Containerization = [[#api|This link jumps to the anchor “api”]]. Containerization is a lightweight form of virtualization that packages an application and its dependencies into a single, portable unit called a container. This approach ensures that software runs consistently across different computing environments, eliminating the classic "it works on my machine" problem. Containers share the host system's kernel, making them more efficient than traditional virtual machines. Modern development workflows rely heavily on containerization to streamline deployment, scaling, and testing. Tools like Docker and Kubernetes have become industry standards, enabling teams to automate infrastructure, orchestrate services, and maintain reliable, reproducible environments. = Exploring API Rate Limiting = API rate limiting is a technique used to control the number of requests a client can make to a server within a specific timeframe. It protects systems from overload, prevents abuse, and ensures fair usage among all clients. Common strategies include fixed windows, sliding windows, and token bucket algorithms. Implementing rate limiting helps maintain system stability and improves user experience by preventing service degradation. Developers often combine rate limiting with monitoring and alerting tools to detect unusual traffic patterns and respond proactively to potential threats or performance issues. Beyond protecting infrastructure, rate limiting also plays a crucial role in API monetization strategies, allowing providers to offer tiered access levels and manage resource allocation efficiently. Additionally, it supports compliance with regulatory requirements by controlling data flow and preventing unauthorized access. As APIs continue to grow in complexity and usage, integrating rate limiting with advanced analytics and machine learning can enhance adaptive control mechanisms, enabling dynamic adjustment of limits based on real-time traffic and threat intelligence. = Future Trends in Containerization and API Management = The landscape of containerization and API management is rapidly evolving, driven by advancements in cloud computing, edge technologies, and artificial intelligence. Organizations are increasingly adopting serverless architectures and microservices, which further emphasize the need for efficient container orchestration and robust API governance. Emerging tools and platforms are focusing on improving security, scalability, and observability. Innovations such as service mesh architectures provide granular control over service-to-service communication, while AI-powered analytics offer predictive insights to optimize resource allocation and detect anomalies early. These trends are shaping the future of how applications are developed, deployed, and managed in dynamic environments. __NOTOC__

{{Info |body = There are different shortcuts to open the layouts macro: “layout”, “columns”, “section”: }} == 1-column layout ==
Think of columns as invisible boxes you drop your content into. One box… two boxes… three — however many makes your info make sense. Instead of stacking everything vertically, you can place things '''side‑by‑side''', which instantly makes your page look more like a polished document and less like a very long shopping list. == 2-column layout ==
[[File:Migration_section-0c811f88-2032-4498-a18c-01364a485cde.jpg|221px|center]] Think of columns as invisible boxes you drop your content into. One box… two boxes… three — however many makes your info make sense. Instead of stacking everything vertically, you can place things side‑by‑side, which instantly makes your page look more like a polished document and less like a very long shopping list.
[[File:Migration_section-a54950cd-95da-46ea-bb37-8dc8648e4530.jpg|223px|center]] Columns are like salt: a little goes a long way.
Use them to bring clarity and style to your page — not to overcomplicate it. Once you get the hang of it, you’ll start dropping layouts everywhere just because they make everything look nicer. If you want, I can also write a more formal version, a funnier version, or a version you can paste directly into a Confluence help page.
== 3-column layout ==

{{ExpandStart |title = Document info |breakoutWidth = 760 }} {| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="ba364474fa1f" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="230a618f-325f-4503-bb47-616484209ed0" ! ac:local-id="1f67c625-b415-4bc3-94fc-7cb41413d915"| Owner | ac:local-id="96e5481f-c72f-4c38-9c11-03081fba957f"| Marketing |- ac:local-id="304c5c51-6c86-44d7-b3f0-5a7e4b208c46" ! ac:local-id="6a1204ef-f0cc-449c-a26e-c481f1a99884"| Document type | ac:local-id="b6c7e713-a90e-4147-830e-44c69915f7a2"| Guideline |} {{ExpandEnd}} These guidelines ensure that everyone communicates about '''PowerKnow''' consistently and professionally. They help maintain a unified brand identity across marketing, sales, product, and customer‑facing materials. == '''Brand Overview''' == === '''Brand Essence''' === '''PowerKnow''' is a smart, intuitive knowledge base platform designed to help teams capture, organize, and share information effortlessly. The brand stands for: * Clarity * Empowerment * Reliability === '''Brand Personality''' === '''PowerKnow''' should feel: * Helpful * Modern * Confident * Human == '''Governance and Ownership''' == * Brand Owner: Marketing * Design Owner: Product Design * Messaging Owner: Product Marketing Review cycle: Every 6 months or after major product updates. == '''Brand Messaging''' == === '''Tagline''' === '''Knowledge that works for you.''' === '''Value Proposition''' === '''PowerKnow''' helps teams capture and share information effortlessly, turning scattered knowledge into a single source of truth. === '''Key Messages''' === * Centralizes company information in one intuitive platform * Reduces onboarding time and eliminates knowledge silos * Built for small teams that need clarity, speed, and collaboration * Simple to start, powerful as you scale === '''Tone of Voice''' === * Clear * Concise * Friendly * Action‑oriented Avoid jargon, overly technical language, or aggressive sales language. == '''Visual Identity''' == === '''Logo Usage''' === '''Primary Logo:''' Wordmark with icon '''Secondary Logo:''' Icon only Rules: * Maintain clear space equal to the height of the “P” * Do not stretch, rotate, recolor, or add effects * Use the primary logo on light backgrounds; use the white version on dark backgrounds === '''Color Palette''' === {| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="8bb66106-3f9e-4013-b7d2-13d5583b6822" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="e0597151-48eb-48a2-bf67-6dc7c0787a95" ! ac:local-id="6e2e5442-5fb4-4906-87f4-37f1ec2cb593"| Color Name ! ac:local-id="0aab7eb1-dd2e-41cd-adc3-5a03b7921f92"| Hex ! ac:local-id="d72ab72d-90c8-4023-aa5f-73a3538dbb70"| Usage |- ac:local-id="4f54ed53-b74e-4938-95c9-acb7beb879cf" | ac:local-id="b03a5146-b840-4de4-81b3-ef69c3fa4f92"| Power Blue | ac:local-id="192eb2d1-be64-42e0-b493-bd0e1d38947d"| #2A6FF2 | ac:local-id="d475eaa3-f47d-49db-8abe-a7e7924d238c"| Buttons, highlights, links |- ac:local-id="cdecd3ae-2676-4f25-b3da-ee5db7f99b50" | ac:local-id="be008a8a-6ae9-4467-851b-071d130c25c3"| Knowledge Navy | ac:local-id="7861d25d-4a69-40a1-ad86-f3daba3c1d0a"| #0F1E3D | ac:local-id="7a4ca50a-d58c-4481-b745-26534b18162a"| Headings, logo text |- ac:local-id="311d96ed-281b-4ec0-988f-56b49a0f904e" | ac:local-id="4b38b000-7382-45b1-9d2d-86b456e88ee4"| Clarity White | ac:local-id="ba6817ad-2864-4a7d-8fab-730ebcff4b5e"| #FFFFFF | ac:local-id="a08650dd-2aad-4ddc-8d26-83f6ae737287"| Backgrounds |- ac:local-id="b55a3de1-f0e9-480d-b9a3-69c9a4d100a9" | ac:local-id="96b3baf5-5110-4e60-890b-d7b2a139f4f8"| Insight Gray | ac:local-id="75a91dd4-a984-462d-8525-ae46a7b6ba91"| #F4F6F8 | ac:local-id="7bf1373d-be21-4ce8-bd67-a5e0f849133c"| Panels, cards, UI elements |- ac:local-id="5af4b04f-d166-400a-ae0b-97301314704a" | ac:local-id="deb48fde-2712-4398-bc32-3fddf7a5c6e2"| Action Green | ac:local-id="c698fe1d-bac5-4396-83c4-173c4db57671"| #2ECC71 | ac:local-id="645b5256-18aa-4f72-8ab7-2e8d7a6dcc3b"| Success states, CTAs |} === '''Typography''' === '''Primary Font:''' Inter * Headings: Inter Bold * Body Text: Inter Regular * UI Labels: Inter Medium Fallbacks: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif === '''Imagery''' === Use imagery that reflects collaboration, simplicity, and modern digital workspaces. Avoid staged stock photos or overly abstract visuals. == '''Product Naming and Terminology''' == === '''Product Name''' === Always: '''PowerKnow''' Never: Power Know, Power-Know, PK === '''Feature Names''' === * Spaces * Pages * Collections * Smart Search * Insights Dashboard === '''Terminology Rules''' === * Use sentence case for UI labels * Avoid internal jargon in customer-facing materials * Use consistent verbs (e.g., “Create,” not “Add new”) == '''Communication Guidelines''' == === '''Marketing''' === * Focus on benefits * Use scannable lists and short paragraphs * Highlight outcomes === '''Sales''' === * Emphasize ROI and efficiency * Use customer stories * Keep messaging aligned with the value proposition === '''Customer Success''' === * Be empathetic and solution-oriented * Use simple, direct instructions * Avoid assumptions about technical knowledge == '''Templates and Examples''' == === '''Email Signature''' === Code Best regards, [Name] [Role] – PowerKnow www.powerknowledge.com === '''Slide Title Example''' === '''PowerKnow''': Your Team’s Single Source of Truth === '''Social Media Example''' === “Knowledge shouldn’t slow you down. '''PowerKnow''' helps teams organize information effortlessly so they can focus on what matters.” __NOTOC__

{{ExpandStart |title = Document info |breakoutWidth = 760 }} {| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="275d6a3a5675" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="7d2a1d82-63aa-4a7d-bb33-76d37a3f5067" ! ac:local-id="96aa91f4-52a7-4273-921d-f447ad68bd08"| Owner | ac:local-id="71469d26-a9cd-4eb6-a945-6f3ac93fd89b"| [[User:624c4eb01da0e100713d309c|624c4eb01da0e100713d309c]] |- ac:local-id="75cbab7f-5110-496c-993b-dc53688faa55" ! ac:local-id="5de954b9-cf41-4351-acd8-33319924f426"| Document type | ac:local-id="b1175ba3-4686-4f09-b28a-f8c36ca54fd2"| SOP |} {{ExpandEnd}}{{Drawio|mVer=2 |zoom=1 |simple=0 |inComment=0 |custContentId=851771415 |pageId=850853890 |lbox=1 |diagramDisplayName=Marketing - Product Launch |contentVer=2 |revision=2 |baseUrl=https://geht.atlassian.net/wiki |diagramName=Migration_SOP_-_Product_Launch-Untitled_Diagram-1773241441802.drawio |pCenter=0 |width=512 |links= |tbstyle= |height=371.5 }} === '''Launch Planned''' === This step marks the formal decision to launch a new product, feature, or major update. Marketing receives the initial information from Product Management, including: * What is being launched * Target release date * High‑level value proposition * Key differentiators '''Outcome:''' Marketing acknowledges the launch and begins preparation. == '''Define Goals and Messaging''' == Marketing clarifies the strategic purpose of the launch and establishes the core narrative. === '''Key activities''' === * Define launch objectives (awareness, adoption, lead generation, customer retention) * Identify primary and secondary audiences * Draft the core messaging pillars * Align messaging with brand guidelines * Validate messaging with Product and Leadership '''Deliverables''' * Messaging brief * Audience definition * Value proposition and key messages == '''Create Launch Plan''' == Marketing builds a structured plan that outlines what will be delivered, when, and by whom. === '''Key activities''' === * Define launch timeline and milestones * Identify required assets (content, visuals, web updates) * Determine channels (email, social, blog, paid, events) * Assign responsibilities within the marketing team * Identify dependencies (e.g., product documentation, design resources) '''Deliverables''' * Launch plan document * Timeline or Gantt chart * Internal kickoff summary == '''Coordinate with Internal Teams''' == Marketing ensures alignment across the organization so the launch is consistent and well‑supported. === '''Key activities''' === * Sync with Product for feature accuracy * Align with Sales on messaging and enablement needs * Coordinate with Customer Success for customer‑facing updates * Confirm availability of design resources * Share the launch plan with all stakeholders '''Deliverables''' * Cross‑team alignment notes * Updated plan based on feedback * Confirmed responsibilities == '''Create Launch Content''' == Marketing develops all written materials needed for the launch. === '''Typical content includes''' === * Blog post or announcement article * Email campaigns (customers, prospects, partners) * Social media posts * Website copy updates * Internal communication draft === '''Key activities''' === * Draft content * Review internally * Incorporate feedback * Finalize copy for design and publishing '''Deliverables''' * Finalized content package * Approved messaging for all channels == '''Prepare Launch Assets''' == Design and marketing collaborate to create the visual components of the launch. === '''Key activities''' === * Create graphics for blog, email, and social * Prepare product screenshots or mockups * Update website visuals if needed * Ensure all assets follow brand guidelines * Deliver final files to marketing '''Deliverables''' * Launch graphics * Social media visuals * Website banners or images * Presentation slides (if needed) == '''Publish Launch Communications''' == Marketing executes the launch across all planned channels. === '''Key activities''' === * Publish blog post or announcement * Send email campaigns * Post on social media channels * Update website pages * Notify internal teams that the launch is live '''Deliverables''' * Live launch content * Public announcement * Updated website == '''Monitor Performance and Report''' == After the launch, marketing evaluates the impact and documents insights. === '''Key activities''' === * Track KPIs (traffic, engagement, conversions, adoption) * Monitor customer and community feedback * Review campaign performance * Identify what worked and what didn’t * Share a post‑launch report with stakeholders '''Deliverables''' * Performance dashboard or report * Recommendations for future launches * Lessons learned summary {{AttachmentsSectionStart}} * [[Media:Migration_SOP_-_Product_Launch-~Untitled_Diagram-1773241441802.drawio.tmp]] * [[Media:Migration_SOP_-_Product_Launch-Untitled_Diagram-1773241441802.drawio.png]] * [[Media:Migration_SOP_-_Product_Launch-Untitled_Diagram-1773241441802.drawio]] {{AttachmentsSectionEnd}} __NOTOC__

This SOP outlines the process the Marketing Team must follow when planning, selecting, and purchasing promotional items for '''Power Knowledge'''. It ensures consistency, cost control, and brand alignment across all merchandise. == '''Purpose''' == To provide a standardized workflow for requesting, evaluating, approving, and purchasing promotional items used for events, campaigns, customer gifts, and internal branding. == '''Scope''' == This procedure applies to all Marketing Team members involved in: * Event planning * Brand awareness campaigns * Customer or partner gifting * Internal swag requests It covers planning, vendor selection, design approval, budgeting, and procurement. == '''Roles and Responsibilities''' == === '''Marketing Manager''' === * Approves promotional item requests * Confirms budget availability * Signs off on final designs === '''Marketing Coordinator''' === * Collects requirements * Researches vendors and pricing * Manages quotes and timelines * Coordinates with designers === '''Finance''' === * Confirms budget allocation * Processes purchase orders and payments === '''Design Team''' === * Creates or reviews artwork * Ensures brand compliance == '''Process Overview''' == === '''Identify the Need''' === A promotional item request can originate from: * Event planning * Sales enablement * Customer success gifting * Internal branding initiatives The requester must provide: * Purpose of the item * Target audience * Quantity needed * Deadline * Budget estimate (if known) Requests are submitted via the Marketing Request Form. === '''Define Requirements''' === The Marketing Coordinator documents: * Item type (e.g., stickers, notebooks, apparel, tech accessories) * Branding requirements * Packaging needs * Shipping or distribution details If the item is new, the Design Team is consulted early. === '''Vendor Research and Quotes''' === The Marketing Coordinator gathers at least '''three vendor options''' unless a preferred vendor already exists. For each vendor, collect: * Unit price * Minimum order quantity * Production timeline * Shipping costs * Print specifications * Sample availability Quotes are stored in the “Promotional Items” folder. === '''Design and Artwork Preparation''' === The Design Team prepares or reviews: * Logo placement * Color usage * Typography * Print-ready files All artwork must follow the '''Power Knowledge Brand Guidelines'''. The Marketing Manager approves the final design. === '''Budget Approval''' === The Marketing Manager submits the selected vendor quote to Finance. Finance confirms: * Budget availability * Cost center allocation * Purchase order requirements No orders may be placed before Finance approval. === '''Place the Order''' === Once approved: * The Marketing Coordinator submits the purchase order * Confirms production timeline * Requests a digital or physical proof * Approves the proof before production begins All confirmations are documented in the project folder. === '''Production and Delivery Tracking''' === The Marketing Coordinator: * Tracks production status * Confirms shipping details * Ensures delivery aligns with event or campaign timelines Any delays must be communicated to stakeholders immediately. === '''Quality Check''' === Upon delivery: * Inspect items for print quality, color accuracy, and defects * Verify quantities * Document any issues and contact the vendor if needed Approved items are stored in the Marketing storage area. === '''Distribution''' === Depending on the purpose: * Event items are shipped to the venue or event owner * Customer gifts are coordinated with Customer Success * Internal items are distributed through Office Management A distribution log is maintained for inventory tracking. === '''Post‑Project Review''' === After each promotional item project: * Evaluate vendor performance * Assess item effectiveness (e.g., event feedback, usage) * Update preferred vendor list * Document learnings for future orders == '''Documentation Requirements''' == The following must be stored in the “Promotional Items” Confluence section: * Request form * Vendor quotes * Approved artwork * Purchase order * Delivery confirmation * Quality check notes * Post‑project review == '''Preferred Vendors''' == A list of approved or recommended vendors is maintained separately and reviewed quarterly. == '''Review Cycle''' == This SOP is reviewed every 12 months or after major changes in procurement or branding processes. __NOTOC__

At Averion Solutions, our Code of Conduct defines the standards of behavior expected from every employee. It reflects our core values—integrity, accountability, respect, and collaboration—and guides how we interact with colleagues, customers, and partners. Following these principles helps create a workplace where everyone can work productively, safely, and with mutual trust. {{Info |body = '''Key Expectations''' * Treat colleagues, customers, and partners with respect * Maintain professional behavior in all communications * Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose potential risks * Use company property responsibly * Represent Averion Solutions professionally in external interactions }} == Professional Behavior == Employees are expected to conduct themselves in a professional and responsible manner at all times. This includes clear communication, punctuality, reliability, and accountability for assigned work. Constructive feedback and open dialogue are encouraged as part of maintaining a collaborative working environment. == Respectful Workplace == Averion Solutions maintains a '''zero-tolerance policy toward harassment, discrimination, or bullying''' of any kind. Employees must ensure that their behavior supports an inclusive workplace where all individuals feel safe, respected, and valued regardless of background, identity, or role. == Conflict of Interest == A conflict of interest occurs when personal interests may interfere with professional responsibilities. Examples may inlcude: {{ExpandStart |title = Examples of Conflicts of Interest |breakoutWidth = 760 }} * Holding financial interests in a supplier or competitor * Outside employment that interferes with job responsibilities * Personal relationships influencing hiring or procurement decisions * Accepting gifts or favors from vendors that may affect business decisions {{ExpandEnd}} Employees should disclose any potential conflict of interest to their manager or HR partner. == Use of Company Property == Company assets—including laptops, software, intellectual property, and internal data—must be used responsibly and primarily for business purposes. Unauthorized use, distribution, or damage to company resources may result in disciplinary action. == Integrity in External Relations == Employees representing Averion Solutions externally must act with professionalism and integrity. This includes meetings with customers, participation in industry events, and communication in public or online spaces. Only authorized departments may issue official company statements. __NOTOC__
