== Overview == The offboarding process at Averion Solutions ensures that employees leaving the organization experience a structured, professional, and transparent exit. Proper offboarding protects company assets, ensures compliance with legal and contractual obligations, and supports a positive relationship with departing employees. Offboarding also allows the organization to capture institutional knowledge, reflect on improvement opportunities, and maintain a strong employer brand. The offboarding process involves collaboration between HR, the employee’s manager, IT, Finance, and other relevant stakeholders to guarantee a smooth and organized transition. == Offboarding Timeline == The offboarding process begins as soon as the employee submits a resignation or when the organization initiates a role termination. The timeline can vary depending on notice periods, role complexity, and handover requirements. '''Typical stages include:''' '''Resignation Submission / Termination Notification'''
Employees formally communicate their intent to leave, or managers initiate termination procedures in compliance with company policy. '''Notice Period Management'''
HR and managers work together to plan work transitions, knowledge transfer, and final deliverables during the employee’s notice period. '''Return of Company Assets & System Access Removal'''
IT, HR Operations, and facilities teams coordinate to recover company property and revoke system access. '''Knowledge Transfer & Documentation'''
Employees provide guidance and documentation to ensure continuity for their team and ongoing projects. '''Exit Interview & Feedback Collection'''
HR conducts a structured conversation to gather insights on the employee experience and identify areas for improvement. == Detailed Offboarding Steps == === Resignation Submission / Termination Notification === Employees are required to submit a resignation in writing to their manager and HR. Standard notice periods, as defined in employment contracts, must be observed. During this stage, HR: * Confirms the employee’s final working date * Shares offboarding procedures and expectations * Coordinates with managers to plan knowledge transfer and transition activities === Notice Period Management === The notice period is a critical phase to ensure operational continuity. During this time, employees are expected to: * Complete ongoing projects or document progress for handover * Train colleagues or successors on key tasks * Attend scheduled transition meetings with managers and relevant stakeholders Managers are responsible for: * Planning workload adjustments to cover responsibilities during the transition * Coordinating with HR to ensure the offboarding checklist is completed * Identifying knowledge gaps and arranging proper handover === Return of Company Assets & System Access === Protecting company assets and sensitive information is a key component of offboarding. IT, HR, and facilities teams collaborate to: * Recover laptops, mobile devices, badges, and other company property * Disable or transfer email, system access, and tool accounts * Ensure compliance with data security policies HR Operations tracks asset return and confirms completion with the employee’s manager. === Knowledge Transfer & Documentation === Offboarding provides an opportunity to capture institutional knowledge and prevent disruption to ongoing work. Employees are asked to: * Document project status, key contacts, and processes * Share files, reports, and access permissions with relevant team members * Mentor or train successors where applicable Managers coordinate with employees to ensure that knowledge transfer is thorough, and the transition plan is documented in internal systems. === Exit Interview & Feedback Collection === Exit interviews are optional but highly encouraged. Conducted by HR, these interviews help the company understand the employee experience and identify potential areas for improvement. Key focus areas include: * Feedback on team dynamics, management, and company culture * Observations on processes, systems, and tools * Reasons for leaving (if voluntarily) and suggestions for improvement * Any outstanding concerns regarding compensation, benefits, or policies Insights from exit interviews are reviewed by HR and leadership to improve retention, onboarding, and overall employee experience. == Roles and Responsibilities == {{ExpandStart |title = Employee |breakoutWidth = 760 }} * Submit resignation in writing and follow notice period requirements * Participate actively in knowledge transfer activities * Return all company property in good condition * Provide honest and constructive feedback during exit interview {{ExpandEnd}}{{ExpandStart |title = Manager |breakoutWidth = 760 }} * Review offboarding plan and transition workload * Coordinate knowledge transfer and project handover * Communicate expectations and timelines to the departing employee * Ensure team continuity during the notice period {{ExpandEnd}}{{ExpandStart |title = HR Business Partner |breakoutWidth = 760 }} * Support employees and managers throughout the offboarding process * Ensure compliance with policies, contracts, and labor laws * Conduct exit interviews and document insights * Coordinate final payroll, benefits, and administrative tasks {{ExpandEnd}}{{ExpandStart |title = HR Operations |breakoutWidth = 760 }} * Track asset return and system access removal * Prepare offboarding documentation and final HR records * Support payroll and benefits processing for departing employees {{ExpandEnd}}{{ExpandStart |title = IT Support |breakoutWidth = 760 }} * Revoke access to systems, applications, and communication tools * Recover hardware and equipment * Ensure company data remains secure after the employee leaves {{ExpandEnd}}{{ExpandStart |title = Finance / Payroll |breakoutWidth = 760 }} * Process final salary, unused PTO payouts, and any applicable bonuses * Confirm benefits continuation or termination based on local regulations * Support tax or legal compliance for the employee’s final pay {{ExpandEnd}}{{PanelStart |panelIcon = :yellow_star: |panelIconId = atlassian-yellow_star |panelIconText = :yellow_star: |bgColor = #FFFAE6 }} == Best Practices for Offboarding == * Start planning as soon as resignation is received to avoid last-minute confusion * Maintain transparency and communicate timelines clearly with all stakeholders * Encourage departing employees to provide candid feedback * Ensure all company assets and sensitive information are secured * Document knowledge transfer thoroughly for operational continuity * Review exit feedback to identify organizational improvements A structured offboarding process ensures employees leave on a positive note while protecting the organization’s interests. {{PanelEnd}} __NOTOC__

== Overview == At '''Averion Solutions''', employee benefits are designed to support wellbeing, professional development, and long-term career growth. Our benefits package reflects the company’s commitment to helping employees maintain a healthy work-life balance while providing opportunities for continuous learning and advancement. This page provides an overview of the resources available to employees. For detailed information, please visit the dedicated pages for each program.
== Benefits == The '''Benefits Overview''' page provides a comprehensive summary of the benefits available to employees, including health coverage, financial benefits, and work-life balance programs. These benefits are designed to support employees throughout different stages of their personal and professional lives. '''Key Areas Covered''' * Health & wellness benefits * Financial and retirement support * Work-life balance programs * Flexible work arrangements ➡ Go to [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/Employee_Resources/Benefits_Overview|Benefits Overview]]
== Learning & Development == The '''Learning & Development''' page outlines the opportunities available to employees to expand their skills and advance their careers at Averion Solutions. Employees are encouraged to actively engage in development programs and work with their managers to define career growth goals. '''Key Areas Covered''' * Training programs and workshops * Online learning and certifications * Mentorship opportunities * Career development planning ➡ Go to [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/Employee_Resources/Learning_and_Development|Learning & Development]]
== How to Access Benefits == Most benefits and development programs are available to employees after onboarding. Specific eligibility requirements may vary depending on: * employment type * location * length of service Employees are encouraged to review the detailed pages for program information or contact HR for clarification.
__NOTOC__

== Overview == Averion Solutions recognizes that '''nutrition plays a critical role in employee wellbeing, energy, and focus'''. The Nutrition & Healthy Eating program is designed to provide employees with '''resources, guidance, and opportunities''' to make informed, healthy food choices—both in the office and at home. Through educational workshops, healthy workplace initiatives, and interactive challenges, employees are empowered to '''fuel their bodies and minds for peak performance'''. == Program Components == === Healthy Workplace Snacks === * Provision of nutritious snacks in break rooms or office kitchens. * Emphasis on fresh fruits, nuts, and low-sugar alternatives. * Optional subscription boxes for remote employees with healthy snacks delivered to home offices. === Nutrition Workshops & Webinars === * Workshops on meal planning, balanced diets, and healthy eating habits. * Webinars covering topics like energy-boosting foods, hydration, and mindful eating. * Led by certified nutritionists or wellness professionals. === Meal Planning Resources === * Guides for quick, nutritious lunches and snacks. * Templates for weekly meal prep and grocery planning. * Tips for maintaining a balanced diet while traveling or working remotely. === Challenges & Initiatives === * Healthy recipe competitions and team cooking challenges. * Hydration challenges or “fruit of the week” initiatives to encourage consistent healthy choices. * Recognition or small incentives for consistent participation. === Personalized Support === * Optional one-on-one consultations with nutrition experts. * Access to apps or trackers for monitoring meals, calories, and water intake. * Tips for accommodating dietary restrictions and preferences. == Employee Resources == * '''Healthy Recipe Library:''' Collection of simple, nutritious recipes. * '''Nutrition Guides:''' Infographics and articles on balanced diets and portion control. * '''Hydration & Snack Trackers:''' Tools to encourage daily water intake and smart snacking. * '''Expert Access:''' Contact details for certified nutritionists or wellness coaches. {{Tip |body = == Participation Tips == * Set realistic nutrition goals (e.g., daily fruit intake, water consumption). * Join workshops or challenges to stay motivated. * Experiment with new recipes or meal planning strategies. * Track progress using apps, journals, or trackers provided by the program. }} __NOTOC__

== Overview == The '''HR Policies''' page provides employees with guidance on the rules, expectations, and procedures that help maintain a fair, safe, and productive work environment at '''Averion Solutions'''. These policies are designed to ensure: * A respectful and ethical workplace * Clarity around employee rights and responsibilities * Compliance with local laws and regulations * Support for flexible and balanced work arrangements Employees should review policies relevant to their role and contact HR with questions. == Key HR Policies == === Code of Conduct === Defines the '''standards of professional behavior''' expected at Averion Solutions, including ethics, workplace respect, and anti-discrimination practices. {{Note |body = See the full [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Policies/Code_of_Conduct|Code of Conduct]] page. }} === Remote Work Policy === Outlines expectations for employees working remotely or in hybrid arrangements, including communication, availability, security, and equipment use. {{Note |body = See the full [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Policies/Remote_Work_Policy|Remote Work Policy]] page. }} === Leave and PTO Policy === Provides details about '''paid time off''', sick leave, parental leave, and other forms of leave available to employees, as well as the process for requesting time off. {{Note |body = See the full [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Policies/Leave_and_PTO_Policy|Leave and PTO Policy]] page. }} == How to Use HR Policies == * Review policies regularly to stay informed about expectations and benefits. * Policies may be updated periodically; check the HR Policies page for the latest versions. * Contact '''HR''' with questions or for clarification on specific policies. * Follow the guidance in the policies to ensure compliance and a positive work environment. __NOTOC__

{{PanelStart |panelIcon = :wave: |panelIconId = 1f44b |panelIconText = 👋 |bgColor = #FFEBE6 }} == Welcome to the HR Team Space! == This space is the central hup for HR policies, processes, templates, and initiatives. Use the sections below to quickly navigate to the information you need. {{PanelEnd}} == Quick Access == * [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Policies|HR Policies]] * [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes|HR Processes]] * [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/Employee_Resources|Employee Resources]] == HR Initiatives == Programs designed to improve employee wellbeing, development, and engagement. {{PageTree|content-title=Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Initiatives|startDepth=1|version-at-save=3}} == HR Operations == Documentation of HR meetings and decisions {{SubpageList|depth=1|allChildren=false|style=|page=Migration:HR/HR Department/HR Minutes|sortAndReverse=|first=10}} __NOTOC__

== Overview == The Benefits Overview page provides employees with a clear and comprehensive view of the benefits available to them at Averion Solutions. This page helps employees understand health, financial, work-life, and wellness benefits, enabling them to make informed decisions and maximize the resources available to support their personal and professional wellbeing. The benefits package reflects the company’s commitment to employee wellbeing, work-life balance, and long-term security. == Benefits Categories == === Health & Wellness === * '''Health Insurance''': Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage for eligible employees. * '''Mental Health Support''': Access to counseling services, mental health professionals, and wellbeing platforms. * '''Wellness Reimbursements''': Employees can claim reimbursement for gym memberships, fitness classes, ergonomic equipment, and mindfulness programs. === Financial & Retirement === * '''Retirement Savings Plan''': Eligible employees can participate in a company-supported retirement plan, with employer contributions depending on location and tenure. * '''Bonuses & Incentives''': Performance-based bonus structures reward individual contributions and company success. * '''Life & Disability Insurance''': Options for short-term or long-term disability and life insurance coverage to protect employees and their families. === Work-Life Balance === * [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Policies/Leave_and_PTO_Policy|Paid Time Off (PTO)]]: Includes vacation days, personal days, and wellness days. PTO allowance increases with tenure. * [[#Sick-Leave|Sick Leave]]: Paid time off to recover from illness or attend medical appointments. * [[#Family-%26-Caregiver-Leave|Parental and Family Leave]]: Paid leave for birth, adoption, or foster care, supporting employees during major family events. * [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Policies/Remote_Work_Policy|Flexible Work Models]]: Remote, hybrid, and flexible work arrangements based on role requirements and team needs. __NOTOC__

== Overview == The Performance Review Cycle at Averion Solutions is a structured process designed to support employee growth, provide constructive feedback, and ensure alignment between individual contributions and company objectives. The goal is to create a transparent, fair, and consistent framework for evaluating performance, recognizing achievements, and identifying development opportunities. Performance reviews are not only about evaluating past work but also about setting clear expectations for the future. They encourage continuous development, promote accountability, and foster a culture of learning and feedback. At Averion Solutions, performance reviews are part of an ongoing dialogue between employees and managers, combining formal evaluation, goal-setting, and informal coaching conversations throughout the year. == Performance Review Timeline == The Performance Review Cycle is conducted annually, with structured touchpoints to ensure alignment and continuous feedback. '''Typical cycle stages:''' '''Goal Setting (Start of the Cycle)'''
Employees and managers define measurable objectives aligned with team and company goals. These objectives provide a clear framework for performance expectations throughout the year. '''Mid-Year Check-In'''
Managers and employees review progress against goals, address challenges, and adjust objectives if necessary. This stage allows for real-time feedback and ensures employees remain on track. '''Year-End Performance Review'''
Employees’ performance is formally evaluated, focusing on goal achievement, demonstrated competencies, and contribution to team and company objectives. Feedback is documented, and performance ratings may influence compensation decisions. == Key Components of the Performance Review Cycle == === Goal Setting === Clear, measurable, and achievable goals are central to a successful review process. Goals are established at the start of each performance cycle and should align with both team objectives and the company’s strategic priorities. Key points: * Goals should be '''SMART''' (Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound) * Managers and employees collaborate to define objectives * Goals may include project deliverables, process improvements, skill development, or contribution to company values * Employees are encouraged to update progress regularly in internal performance tools === Continuous Feedback and Coaching === Performance management is an ongoing process rather than a single annual evaluation. Managers are encouraged to provide frequent, constructive feedback to support employee growth. Typical practices: * Weekly or bi-weekly check-ins to discuss progress, challenges, and priorities * Feedback focused on behaviors, skills, and results * Recognition of achievements and contributions in real-time * Open dialogue where employees can request feedback on specific tasks or projects === Mid-Year Check-In === The mid-year check-in provides an opportunity to reflect on progress, adjust goals if necessary, and ensure employees are aligned with evolving team or company priorities. Typical mid-year check-in activities: * Review goals set at the beginning of the cycle * Discuss challenges and opportunities for improvement * Identify training or development needs * Adjust objectives as needed to reflect shifting priorities This touchpoint ensures that employees are supported in meeting their objectives and that potential issues are addressed proactively. === Year-End Performance Review === The year-end review is a formal evaluation of employee performance, incorporating both qualitative and quantitative assessments. This process considers: * Achievement of goals and deliverables * Demonstration of company values (Collaboration, Ownership, Inclusion, Growth Mindset, Wellbeing) * Strengths, development areas, and potential growth opportunities * Feedback from managers, peers, and cross-functional colleagues (when applicable) The outcome of the year-end review may influence decisions related to: * Compensation adjustments or bonuses * Promotions or role changes * Learning and development opportunities == Roles and Responsibilities == {{ExpandStart |title = Employee |breakoutWidth = 760 }} * Actively participate in goal setting and review discussions * Document achievements, challenges, and learnings throughout the cycle * Seek feedback proactively and apply it to improve performance * Prepare for mid-year and year-end discussions {{ExpandEnd}}{{ExpandStart |title = Manager |breakoutWidth = 760 }} * Set clear performance expectations and align goals with team objectives * Provide continuous feedback and coaching throughout the year * Conduct mid-year and year-end review meetings * Identify development opportunities and recommend training resources {{ExpandEnd}}{{ExpandStart |title = HR Business Partner |breakoutWidth = 760 }} * Oversee compliance with performance management policies * Provide guidance and tools for goal-setting and evaluation * Ensure consistency in performance ratings and calibration across teams * Support managers in addressing performance challenges {{ExpandEnd}} == Tools and Resources == To support the Performance Review Cycle, Averion Solutions provides: * '''Performance management system''': for documenting goals, tracking progress, and recording feedback * '''Templates''': for goal-setting, self-assessment, and manager evaluations * '''Guides and training''': on giving constructive feedback, conducting effective reviews, and development planning These tools help employees and managers stay organized, ensure transparency, and maintain a structured approach to performance management. {{PanelStart |panelIcon = :yellow_star: |panelIconId = atlassian-yellow_star |panelIconText = :yellow_star: |bgColor = #FFFAE6 }} == Best Practices for Success == * Start goal-setting discussions early and involve employees in the process * Use the performance management system consistently to track progress * Provide feedback that is specific, actionable, and tied to objectives * Schedule regular check-ins, even outside formal review cycles * Encourage a growth mindset and focus on development opportunities By following these practices, the Performance Review Cycle becomes a valuable tool for career growth, engagement, and alignment with company objectives. {{PanelEnd}} __NOTOC__

== Overview == The Mentorship Program at Averion Solutions is designed to foster '''professional growth, knowledge sharing, and career development'''. By pairing experienced employees (mentors) with colleagues looking to develop their skills and navigate their career paths (mentees), we create a culture of '''continuous learning, collaboration, and personal development'''. The program supports both '''new hires''' during onboarding and '''existing employees''' seeking guidance for career advancement or skill growth. == Program Objectives == '''Skill Development''' – Help mentees build technical, leadership, and soft skills through structured guidance. '''Knowledge Transfer''' – Preserve institutional knowledge and best practices across teams. '''Career Pathing''' – Support mentees in identifying career goals and creating actionable development plans. '''Networking & Collaboration''' – Strengthen internal relationships across departments and foster cross-functional learning. '''Employee Engagement''' – Enhance satisfaction, retention, and overall workplace wellbeing by providing meaningful development opportunities. == Program Structure == === Mentor Selection === * Mentors are typically senior employees with '''relevant experience, leadership skills, and a willingness to coach'''. * HR coordinates mentor nominations and matches based on '''career goals, skill sets, and department alignment'''. === Mentee Enrollment === * Mentees can apply to join the program at the beginning of each quarter. * Employees are encouraged to '''define clear learning objectives''' prior to being paired with a mentor. === Duration & Meetings === * Standard mentorship cycles last '''3–6 months''', depending on objectives. * Meetings occur '''bi-weekly or monthly''' and are guided by agreed-upon development goals. * Mentors and mentees use '''a shared progress tracker''' to monitor outcomes and adjust focus areas. == Roles and Responsibilities == {{ExpandStart |title = Mentor |breakoutWidth = 760 }} * Provide guidance, support, and constructive feedback to the mentee. * Share expertise, knowledge, and best practices relevant to the mentee’s development goals. * Help mentees navigate internal processes, tools, and opportunities for growth. * Encourage independent thinking and problem-solving. {{ExpandEnd}}{{ExpandStart |title = Mentee |breakoutWidth = 760 }} * Take ownership of personal development and actively participate in mentorship sessions. * Set specific, measurable learning objectives and communicate progress with the mentor. * Apply feedback, practice new skills, and reflect on experiences. * Provide constructive feedback to mentors to improve the program. {{ExpandEnd}}{{ExpandStart |title = HR Business Partner |breakoutWidth = 760 }} * Coordinate mentor-mentee matching and monitor program participation. * Provide resources, templates, and guidance for mentorship sessions. * Collect feedback from participants to improve program effectiveness. * Track program metrics and report outcomes to leadership. {{ExpandEnd}} == Program Resources == * [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Initiatives/Career_Development_and_Learning/Mentorship_Program/Mentorship_Guidelines|Mentorship Guidelines]] – A document outlining roles, expectations, and suggested session structures. * [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Initiatives/Career_Development_and_Learning/Mentorship_Program/Development_Plan_Template|Development Plan Template]] – A template to define goals, milestones, and expected outcomes. * [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Initiatives/Career_Development_and_Learning/Mentorship_Program/Progress_Tracker_Template|Progress Tracker]] – Optional Confluence table or shared sheet to monitor ongoing mentorship progress. * '''Workshops & Training''' – Sessions for mentors and mentees to improve coaching skills and maximize program effectiveness. {{PanelStart |panelIcon = :yellow_star: |panelIconId = atlassian-yellow_star |panelIconText = :yellow_star: |bgColor = #FFFAE6 }} == Best Practices == * Set '''clear expectations''' at the start of the mentorship cycle. * Encourage '''open and honest communication''' between mentor and mentee. * Regularly review goals and adjust the mentorship plan as needed. * Recognize and celebrate achievements or milestones completed during the program. * Gather feedback at the end of the cycle to '''continuously improve the program'''. {{PanelEnd}} __NOTOC__

== Overview == The onboarding process at Averion Solutions is designed to help new employees integrate smoothly into the organization and begin contributing effectively in their roles. A structured onboarding experience ensures that new team members feel welcomed, understand company expectations, and gain access to the tools and resources required for success. Successful onboarding goes beyond administrative tasks. It introduces employees to the company’s culture, values, and working environment while helping them build relationships with colleagues and managers. At Averion Solutions, onboarding is a collaborative effort involving HR, hiring managers, IT support, and team members. The onboarding journey typically begins before the employee’s first day and continues throughout the first several weeks of employment. The onboarding process consists of three main stages: * Pre-Onboarding Preparation * First Week Experience * First 30 Days Integration Each stage plays an important role in helping employees transition successfully into their new position. == Pre-Onboarding Preparation == Pre-onboarding begins once a candidate accepts their employment offer. The goal of this stage is to ensure that all administrative, technical, and logistical preparations are completed before the employee’s first day. Proper preparation helps create a positive first impression and ensures that the new hire can begin working productively from day one. During this stage, HR coordinates onboarding activities with internal teams, including HR Operations, IT Support, and the hiring manager. === Pre-Onboarding Checklist ===
[] Prepare employment contract and documentation – @HR Business Partner
[] Register employee in HR and payroll systems – @HR Operations
[] Request laptop and equipment from IT – @HR Operations
[] Provision system and tool accounts – @IT Support
[] Assign onboarding buddy – @Hiring Manager
[] Schedule first-week meetings and onboarding sessions – @Hiring Manager
Once these steps are completed, the new employee receives onboarding instructions, access information, and details about their first working day. == First Day Orientation == The employee’s first day is focused on welcoming them to the organization and providing an introduction to the company’s mission, values, and operations. Managers and HR work together to ensure that the employee has a clear understanding of their role, team structure, and initial priorities. Typical first-day activities include: * Welcome meeting with the hiring manager * Introduction to the team * Overview of the company’s mission and organizational structure * Setup of collaboration and communication tools * Review of workplace policies and internal resources During this stage, employees are encouraged to ask questions and familiarize themselves with the company’s systems and communication channels. A well-structured first day helps reduce uncertainty and helps new employees feel comfortable in their new environment. == First Week Experience == The first week is designed to provide employees with the information and connections they need to begin contributing to their team’s work. This period focuses on introducing employees to company processes, products, and tools while helping them establish working relationships with colleagues. Managers typically organize meetings with key stakeholders and provide guidance on the employee’s first assignments. === First Week Onboarding Checklist ===
[] Conduct welcome meeting and role overview – @Hiring Manager
[] Introduce employee to immediate team members – @Hiring Manager
[] Review onboarding plan and learning goals – @Hiring Manager
[] Complete IT and security setup verification – @IT Support
[] Introduce company tools and collaboration platforms – @IT Support
[] Schedule introductory meetings with key stakeholders – @Hiring Manager
[] Meet with onboarding buddy for informal guidance – @Onboarding Buddy
The goal of the first week is to ensure that the employee understands their responsibilities and feels comfortable navigating company tools and internal communication channels. == Buddy Program == Each new employee is paired with an onboarding buddy who helps them adjust to the workplace during their first weeks. The buddy is typically a colleague from the same team or department who is familiar with internal processes and daily workflows. The buddy’s role is informal but important. They help new employees feel comfortable asking practical questions that may not be covered during formal onboarding sessions. Typical buddy responsibilities include: * Introducing the new employee to team routines and practices * Answering questions about tools, communication channels, and processes * Helping the employee navigate internal resources * Encouraging participation in team discussions and activities The buddy program supports Averion Solutions’ culture of collaboration and helps new employees feel connected to their team early in their employment. == First 30 Days Integration == During the first month, employees gradually transition from orientation activities to fully participating in their team’s work. Managers provide guidance, assign initial tasks, and check in regularly to ensure that the employee is progressing comfortably in their role. The first 30 days focus on learning, exploration, and relationship building. Key goals during this phase include: * Understanding team workflows and responsibilities * Gaining familiarity with internal tools and systems * Completing initial training programs * Beginning contributions to team projects Managers are encouraged to provide regular feedback and clarify expectations to support a smooth transition. == Onboarding Review and Feedback == At the end of the onboarding period, managers conduct a check-in with the employee to review progress and address any remaining questions. This conversation typically includes: * Review of the employee’s first experiences in the role * Discussion of initial performance goals * Identification of training or development opportunities * Feedback on the onboarding experience Employee feedback is valuable for improving onboarding processes and identifying opportunities to enhance the new hire experience. == Roles and Responsibilities == Successful onboarding requires collaboration between several stakeholders. {{ExpandStart |title = Hiring Manager |breakoutWidth = 760 }} The Hiring Manager plays the most important role in ensuring a successful onboarding experience. Beyond selecting the candidate during the recruitment process, the manager is responsible for helping the new employee integrate into the team and understand their responsibilities. Before the employee’s first day, the hiring manager prepares the team for the new arrival and ensures that key onboarding activities are scheduled. This preparation helps create a welcoming and organized first-day experience. During the first week, the hiring manager focuses on introducing the employee to the team, explaining expectations, and providing an overview of the projects or responsibilities the employee will work on. Typical responsibilities of the Hiring Manager include: * Assigning an '''onboarding buddy''' who will support the employee during the first weeks * Scheduling '''first-week meetings and onboarding sessions''' * Conducting a '''welcome meeting''' and reviewing the employee’s role and responsibilities * Introducing the employee to team members and key stakeholders * Providing an overview of team workflows, tools, and communication practices * Assigning initial tasks or learning activities * Checking in regularly to answer questions and provide feedback The hiring manager is also responsible for ensuring that the employee understands performance expectations and feels supported during the early stages of their role. {{ExpandEnd}}{{ExpandStart |title = HR Business Partner |breakoutWidth = 760 }} The HR Business Partner oversees the onboarding process from a people and policy perspective. Their role is to ensure that the onboarding experience follows company guidelines while supporting both the hiring manager and the new employee. HR Business Partners help coordinate onboarding activities and ensure that employees receive all necessary documentation and information before starting their role. A key responsibility of HR during onboarding is preparing the formal employment documentation and ensuring that contractual agreements comply with company policies and applicable employment regulations. Typical responsibilities of the HR Business Partner include: * Preparing '''employment contracts and onboarding documentation''' * Ensuring compliance with company policies and employment regulations * Providing guidance to hiring managers on onboarding best practices * Supporting employees with questions related to policies, benefits, or employment conditions * Ensuring that onboarding activities align with company culture and values * Acting as a point of contact for any HR-related concerns during the onboarding period HR Business Partners also help ensure that the onboarding process reflects the company’s values of collaboration, transparency, and employee support. {{ExpandEnd}}{{ExpandStart |title = HR Operations |breakoutWidth = 760 }} The HR Operations team manages the administrative and system-related tasks that enable a smooth onboarding experience. Their work ensures that new employees are correctly registered in internal systems and can access the necessary organizational resources. Once a candidate accepts their offer, HR Operations begins the technical onboarding process by creating employee records and coordinating with other departments. Typical responsibilities of HR Operations include: * Registering the new employee in '''HR and payroll systems''' * Creating and maintaining '''employee records''' * Coordinating onboarding logistics and internal communication * Submitting requests to IT for equipment and system setup * Ensuring that employee data is accurately recorded and securely stored * Supporting the preparation of onboarding materials and documentation These administrative steps are critical for ensuring that employees are properly integrated into company systems before their first day. {{ExpandEnd}}{{ExpandStart |title = IT Support |breakoutWidth = 760 }} The IT Support team is responsible for ensuring that new employees have access to the technology and tools required to perform their work effectively. IT collaborates closely with HR Operations to prepare technical resources before the employee’s start date. A well-prepared IT setup allows employees to begin working productively without delays. Typical responsibilities of IT Support include: * Preparing '''company-issued laptops and equipment''' * Setting up '''email accounts and communication tools''' * Provisioning access to internal systems and collaboration platforms * Configuring security requirements such as authentication and device management * Providing initial technical guidance to new employees if necessary * Ensuring compliance with company cybersecurity and data protection policies By completing these tasks before the employee’s first day, IT Support helps create a smooth technical onboarding experience. {{ExpandEnd}}{{ExpandStart |title = Onboarding Buddy |breakoutWidth = 760 }} The onboarding buddy plays an informal but important role in helping new employees feel comfortable within the organization. The buddy acts as a friendly point of contact who can answer day-to-day questions and provide guidance on how things work within the team. Buddies are typically colleagues who are familiar with the team’s workflows and culture and who can help new employees navigate their early days at the company. Typical responsibilities of the onboarding buddy include: * Welcoming the employee and introducing them to team members * Explaining team routines, communication channels, and collaboration tools * Helping the employee understand informal practices and workplace norms * Answering practical questions about internal processes * Encouraging the employee to participate in meetings and team discussions * Checking in regularly during the first weeks to offer support The buddy program reflects Averion Solutions’ culture of collaboration and helps create a welcoming environment where employees feel comfortable asking questions and learning from their peers. {{ExpandEnd}} == Continuous Improvement == The onboarding process is reviewed regularly to ensure it remains effective, scalable, and aligned with the needs of the organization. Feedback from new employees, managers, and HR teams helps identify opportunities to improve onboarding materials, training resources, and internal coordination. By continuously refining the onboarding experience, Averion Solutions ensures that new employees feel supported, prepared, and confident as they begin their journey with the company. __NOTOC__

== Overview == Averion Solutions is committed to fostering continuous professional growth. The Learning & Development page provides guidance on the programs, resources, and support available to employees to enhance their skills, advance their careers, and achieve personal development goals. Employees are encouraged to take ownership of their development and work with managers to align learning opportunities with career objectives. == Learning Opportunities == === Training Programs === * '''Workshops & Seminars''': Role-specific skills, soft skills, and leadership development. * '''E-Learning & Certifications''': Access to online courses, certifications, and technical skill programs. * '''Cross-Functional Learning''': Opportunities to participate in projects or programs outside of the employee’s primary role to build new competencies. === Development Support === * '''Education Reimbursement''': Employees may apply for reimbursement for job-relevant courses or certifications. * '''Career Pathing''': Managers work with employees to create development plans and identify potential advancement opportunities. * '''Mentorship Programs''': Pairing with experienced colleagues to receive guidance, career advice, and coaching. === Feedback & Growth === * '''Performance Reviews & Goal Setting''': Learning objectives are integrated with the performance review process to ensure alignment with company expectations. * '''Coaching & Continuous Feedback''': Employees are encouraged to request and provide feedback regularly to support ongoing development. __NOTOC__

== Remote Work Policy == Averion Solutions supports flexible working arrangements, including remote and hybrid work models, where business needs allow. Remote work enables employees to maintain productivity while balancing personal and professional responsibilities. To ensure effective collaboration and data security, employees working remotely must follow the guidelines outlined below. == Eligibility == Remote work availability may vary depending on role requirements, project responsibilities, and team collaboration needs. Managers work with employees to determine appropriate remote or hybrid arrangements. == Remote Work Expectations == Employees working remotely are expected to: * Maintain reliable internet access * Ensure a quiet and professional working environment * Be available during agreed-upon '''core collaboration hours''' * Participate in team meetings and required check-ins * Keep calendars and availability updated == Communication & Collaboration == Remote employees must remain reachable through approved communication channels, including email, messaging platforms, and scheduled meetings. Teams should establish clear expectations for availability and response times to maintain smooth collaboration across locations. == Data Security Requirements == Employees must follow all IT and data security policies when working remotely. Examples include: * Using company-approved devices * Accessing systems via secure connections or VPN * Avoiding the use of public or unsecured networks * Protecting confidential information from unauthorized access __NOTOC__

== HR Processes at Averion Solutions == Human Resources processes at '''Averion Solutions''' are designed to support employees throughout their entire journey with the company—from the moment a role is opened to the final stage of offboarding. Our HR processes ensure that employee-related activities are '''consistent, transparent, and aligned with company values'''. By following structured workflows, we create a predictable experience for employees, managers, and HR partners while maintaining compliance with internal policies and regional regulations. These processes also help reinforce our core cultural principles: * '''Collaboration First''' – HR works closely with managers and teams to support effective hiring, onboarding, and development. * '''Ownership & Accountability''' – Managers and employees actively participate in people processes rather than relying solely on HR. * '''Growth Mindset''' – Continuous feedback and development planning help employees grow throughout their careers. * '''Wellbeing Matters''' – Structured processes support fair workloads, work-life balance, and employee support. The following sections outline the key HR workflows that guide employee management and development within the organization. {{PageTree|content-title={{FULLPAGENAME}}}} __NOTOC__

Averion Solutions recognizes the importance of rest, personal time, and flexibility. Our leave policies are designed to support employee wellbeing and allow time for personal, family, and health-related needs. The following sections outline the primary types of leave available to employees. == Paid Time Off (PTO) == Full-time employees receive a yearly allocation of Paid Time Off that can be used for vacation, personal time, or wellness days. PTO allowances may increase based on tenure and regional employment regulations. Employees should submit PTO requests through the HR system and coordinate scheduling with their manager in advance. == Sick Leave == Employees who are unable to work due to illness or medical appointments should notify their manager as soon as possible. Depending on local regulations, a medical certificate may be required for extended absences. == Family & Caregiver Leave == Employees may request leave for major life events such as: * Caring for a family member * Childbirth or adoption * Family medical situations Eligibility and compensation depend on regional employment laws and company policy. {{ExpandStart |title = Example Leave Scenarios |breakoutWidth = 760 }} '''Example 1: Planned Vacation''' An employee submits a two-week PTO request through the HR portal at least four weeks in advance to ensure adequate team coverage. '''Example 2: Sick Leave''' An employee wakes up ill and informs their manager via messaging before the start of the workday. '''Example 3: Family Leave''' Employees experiencing the loss of an immediate family member may take bereavement leave to manage personal matters and grieve. Additional compassionate leave may be arranged in coordination with HR. {{ExpandEnd}}{{Note |body = You can find the template to request PTO / Leave [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Policies/Leave_and_PTO_Policy/Leave_Request,_Approval_Form_Template|here]]. }} __NOTOC__

== Overview == At Averion Solutions, workplace wellbeing is a priority. The '''Workplace Wellbeing Program''' is designed to support employees’ physical, mental, and emotional health, fostering a positive, productive, and resilient workforce. Our wellbeing initiatives focus on '''holistic support''', offering resources and programs that promote health, balance, and engagement. [[File:Migration_Workplace_Wellbeing_Program-EUlitjXd-20260313-141235.jpg|300px|left]] == [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Initiatives/Wellbeing_and_Workplace_Support/Workplace_Wellbeing_Program/Physical_Health_and_Fitness|Physical Health & Fitness]] == Employees are encouraged to stay active and maintain physical wellness. Programs may include gym memberships, group fitness classes, virtual workout sessions, and on-site wellness challenges. [[File:Migration_Workplace_Wellbeing_Program-R592BoD4-20260313-141247.jpg|300px|right]] == [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Initiatives/Wellbeing_and_Workplace_Support/Workplace_Wellbeing_Program/Mental_Health_and_Mindfullness|Mental Health & Mindfulness]] == Supporting mental wellbeing is a core focus. Programs include access to confidential counseling, mindfulness workshops, meditation sessions, and mental health awareness campaigns. [[File:Migration_Workplace_Wellbeing_Program-trmZSc7T-20260313-141302.jpg|300px|left]] == [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Initiatives/Wellbeing_and_Workplace_Support/Workplace_Wellbeing_Program/Nutrition_and_Healthy_Eating|Nutrition & Healthy Eating]] == Healthy eating initiatives help employees maintain energy and focus. Programs may include nutrition workshops, healthy snack options in break rooms, and online resources for meal planning. [[File:Migration_Workplace_Wellbeing_Program-grafik-20260313-142219.png|300px|right]] == [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Initiatives/Wellbeing_and_Workplace_Support/Workplace_Wellbeing_Program/Work-Life_Balance_and_Flexible_Work|Work-Life Balance & Flexible Work]] == We support employees in balancing personal and professional life. Initiatives include flexible work schedules, remote work options, wellness days, and time management workshops. [[File:Migration_Workplace_Wellbeing_Program-9hPynmQo-20260313-141910.jpg|300px|left]] == [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Initiatives/Wellbeing_and_Workplace_Support/Workplace_Wellbeing_Program/Social_and_Team_Wellbeing|Social & Team Wellbeing]] == Building strong workplace relationships is essential. Programs include team-building events, social clubs, volunteer activities, and peer recognition programs. [[File:Migration_Workplace_Wellbeing_Program-Ca6ry12j-20260313-141931.jpg|300px|right]] == [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Initiatives/Wellbeing_and_Workplace_Support/Workplace_Wellbeing_Program/Social_and_Team_Wellbeing|Financial Wellbeing]] == Financial health is part of overall wellbeing. Initiatives may include workshops on budgeting, retirement planning, savings programs, and access to financial advisors. __NOTOC__

The '''hiring process''' at Averion Solutions is designed to '''attract, evaluate, and onboard talented individuals who align with our technical requirements and company values.''' This page serves as a central hub for all hiring-related processes, providing guidance, templates, and operational best practices for hiring managers, HR partners, and interviewers. '''You can find a detailed overview of the workflow''' [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Processes/Hiring_Processes/Hiring_Workflow|here]]'''.''' == Hiring Lifecycle == {{Drawio|mVer=2 |zoom=1 |simple=0 |inComment=0 |custContentId=864288796 |pageId=855932944 |lbox=1 |diagramDisplayName=Untitled Diagram-1773741262744.drawio |contentVer=2 |revision=2 |baseUrl=https://geht.atlassian.net/wiki |diagramName=Migration_Hiring_Processes-Untitled_Diagram-1773741262744.drawio |pCenter=0 |width=851 |links= |tbstyle= |height=141 }} == Related Pages == {{PageTree|content-title={{FULLPAGENAME}}}} == Roles and Responsiblities Summary == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="70210221-db3b-4a7c-b397-8ddd28dbe6ee" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="d32c69b4651b" ! ac:local-id="0be264c2e2f3"| '''Stage''' ! ac:local-id="31b573543c8d"| '''Responsible Parties''' |- ac:local-id="b475a16d8026" | ac:local-id="073576caeea9"| Role Approval | ac:local-id="6928c3f902a3"| Hiring Manager, HR, Finance, Department Director |- ac:local-id="986a64d65767" | ac:local-id="d3660a9e4eba"| Job Description | ac:local-id="2963c8d8ed9d"| Hiring Manager, HR |- ac:local-id="386db208a6ca" | ac:local-id="8013e681c9bd"| Sourcing and Screening | ac:local-id="d1876f40593b"| HR, Hiring Manager |- ac:local-id="8397ada97357" | ac:local-id="8397e91566b2"| Evaluation and Decision | ac:local-id="c14a5fd9e543"| Hiring Manager, Interview Panel, HR |- ac:local-id="224686ccd364" | ac:local-id="29b21fba4d71"| Offer and Pre-Onboarding | ac:local-id="e4e7a678fd72"| HR, Hiring Manager, IT, Finance |} == Best Practices and Tips == * Maintain clear documentation at every stage * Ensure consistent candidate experience * Involve the right stakeholders early to avoid delays * Keep processes flexible enough for role-specific needs {{AttachmentsSectionStart}} * [[Media:Migration_Hiring_Processes-Untitled_Diagram-1773741262744.drawio.png]] * [[Media:Migration_Hiring_Processes-Untitled_Diagram-1773741262744.drawio]] * [[Media:Migration_Hiring_Processes-~Untitled_Diagram-1773741262744.drawio.tmp]] {{AttachmentsSectionEnd}} __NOTOC__

{{Info |body = Other shortcuts for columns are “layout” or “[[Migration:Macros/Supported_macros/section|section]]”. }}
== Overview == A well‑organized knowledge base helps teams access information quickly. By centralizing documentation, organizations reduce duplicated effort and ensure that updates reach everyone at the same time. A clear structure also helps the onboarding of new team members.
== Processes == Documented processes ensure that tasks are completed consistently, regardless of who performs them. This reduces errors, speeds up execution, and makes it easier to train new contributors. Standardized workflows also help teams measure performance.
== Support == A support section gives users a quick path to resolving issues. Clear steps, expected outcomes, and escalation paths help reduce support load and empower users to solve problems independently. When new issues arise, adding them promptly keeps the knowledge base relevant.
__NOTOC__

== Launch Planned ==
[x] Confirm launch scope with Product
[x] Confirm target release date
[x] Gather initial product details
[x] Identify key stakeholders
[] Create internal launch folder for planning and assets
== Define Goals and Messaging ==
[] Define launch objectives (awareness, adoption, leads, retention)
[] Identify primary and secondary audiences
[] Draft value proposition
[] Create messaging pillars
[] Validate messaging with Product and Leadership
[] Finalize messaging brief
== Create Launch Plan ==
[] Build launch timeline with milestones
[] Identify required content (blog, email, social, website)
[] Identify required assets (graphics, screenshots, visuals)
[] Define channels and distribution strategy
[] Assign responsibilities within Marketing
[] Identify dependencies (design, product info, approvals)
[] Share plan with stakeholders
== Coordinate with Internal Teams ==
[] Align with Product on feature accuracy
[] Align with Sales on enablement needs
[] Align with Customer Success on customer communication
[] Confirm design resource availability
[] Update plan based on feedback
[] Finalize internal alignment
== Create Launch Content ==
[] Draft blog post or announcement
[] Draft email campaigns
[] Draft social media posts
[] Draft website copy updates
[] Review content internally
[] Apply revisions
[] Finalize all written content
== Prepare Launch Assets ==
[] Request required visuals from Design
[] Provide messaging and content to Design
[] Review draft visuals
[] Request revisions if needed
[] Approve final assets
[] Prepare files for publishing (correct formats, sizes)
== Publish Launch Communications ==
[] Publish blog post
[] Send email campaigns
[] Post on social channels
[] Update website pages
[] Notify internal teams that launch is live
== Monitor Performance and Report ==
[] Track KPIs (traffic, engagement, conversions, adoption)
[] Monitor customer feedback
[] Review campaign performance
[] Document insights and lessons learned
[] Share post-launch report with stakeholders
__NOTOC__

== Overview == Averion Solutions recognizes that '''financial health is a key component of overall employee wellbeing'''. Our Financial Wellbeing program is designed to provide employees with '''resources, guidance, and tools''' to manage personal finances, plan for the future, and make informed decisions that reduce stress and improve confidence. The program empowers employees to '''achieve financial stability, set goals, and make smart financial choices''' while balancing work and life responsibilities. == Program Components == === Financial Education Workshops === * Interactive workshops on budgeting, saving, investing, and debt management. * Webinars covering topics like retirement planning, tax optimization, and managing major life expenses. * Led by certified financial advisors or internal experts. === Personalized Financial Guidance === * Optional one-on-one financial consultations to create customized plans. * Access to tools and calculators for budgeting, retirement, and savings goals. * Advice on employee benefits, compensation planning, and investment options. === Savings & Retirement Programs === * Guidance and support for company-sponsored retirement plans and savings programs. * Education on employer contributions, matching programs, and tax-advantaged accounts. * Regular updates and reminders to maximize participation and benefits. === Incentives & Recognition === * Programs to encourage responsible financial practices, such as savings challenges or goal achievements. * Recognition for employees who complete financial education modules or milestones. * Access to rewards or incentives tied to participation in financial wellbeing initiatives. === Digital Tools & Resources === * Access to budgeting apps, financial trackers, and planning templates. * Guides, infographics, and articles on topics such as emergency funds, debt reduction, and investing. * Curated resources for different stages of financial planning—from early career to pre-retirement. == Employee Resources == * '''Financial Education Portal:''' Workshops, articles, and video tutorials on personal finance topics. * '''Budgeting Tools & Templates:''' Resources to track expenses, savings, and investments. * '''Retirement Planning Guides:''' Information on company-supported retirement programs and external resources. * '''Consultation Access:''' Contact details for financial advisors and internal HR partners. {{Tip |body = == Participation Tips == * Set realistic financial goals for short-term and long-term planning. * Participate in workshops and explore budgeting and planning tools. * Take advantage of retirement programs, savings incentives, and employer contributions. * Review personal finances regularly and adjust plans as needed. }} __NOTOC__

== Overview == HR Initiatives at Averion Solutions are programs designed to support employee wellbeing, professional development, and a positive workplace culture. These initiatives aim to improve the employee experience, strengthen collaboration across teams, and support continuous learning and growth within the organization. Each initiative has a dedicated page with detailed information, resources, and participation guidelines.HR Initiatives at '''Averion Solutions''' are programs designed to support employee wellbeing, professional development, and a positive workplace culture. These initiatives focus on improving the employee experience, strengthening collaboration across teams, and supporting continuous learning and growth within the organization. Each initiative has its own dedicated page with detailed information, resources, and participation guidelines. == Categories of HR Initiatives == === [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Initiatives/Wellbeing_and_Workplace_Support|Wellbeing & Workplace Support]] === Programs that focus on physical, mental, and social wellbeing to help employees thrive at work. === [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Initiatives/Career_Development_and_Learning|Career Development & Learning]] === Initiatives that help employees grow professionally, expand skills, and build career paths. === [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Initiatives/Recognition_and_Engagement|Recognition & Engagement]] === Programs that celebrate employee achievements and foster a positive workplace culture. === [[Migration:HR/HR_Department/HR_Initiatives/Diversity,_Equity,_and_Inclusion|Diversity, Equity & Inclusion (DEI)]] === Programs to foster an inclusive, equitable, and diverse workplace. == How New Initiatives Are Introduced == New HR initiatives are typically introduced when the organization identifies opportunities to improve employee wellbeing, engagement, or development. The HR team may: * run pilot programs * collect employee feedback * review program effectiveness before expanding initiatives company-wide. Updates about new initiatives are communicated through: * internal announcements * HR meetings {{PanelStart |panelIcon = :yellow_star: |panelIconId = atlassian-yellow_star |panelIconText = :yellow_star: |bgColor = #FFFAE6 }} === '''Best Practices''' === * Assign clear '''initiative owners''' (HR partner or program lead) * Provide '''templates and resources''' for employee participation * Communicate updates consistently via internal channels * Track effectiveness through surveys, participation metrics, and feedback {{PanelEnd}} __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 26 August 2024 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="dd7e91c6-3e1a-4664-816e-913d4d379afd" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="2b95241e-3c4a-4e97-85a1-52e525a82d59" ! ac:local-id="e3b23944-c51c-4935-873d-5ca0f7c07b73"| '''Action''' ! ac:local-id="e682fa7c-5bf6-4fd3-8a2f-df47695fdabd"| '''Owner''' ! ac:local-id="86811d79-cc87-4f89-b9ba-ad935dbcf759"| '''Deadline''' ! ac:local-id="5931a9c2-c4ed-40e1-af1e-41374e9be54b"| '''Status''' |- ac:local-id="e3f89ae5-a33c-4a57-84c1-e7ff2da53d7d" | ac:local-id="29ec7243-6277-4118-bfd3-ab0ba7456748"| Send weekly recruitment status updates to hiring managers | ac:local-id="91291089-1aa5-4cf7-a0a7-8b344a0030cc"| MK | ac:local-id="99e1e61d-7cf2-40bc-8cdb-85adf0accef6"| Starting next Monday | ac:local-id="8c5e02b2-d47d-4527-92ba-c35cb585952e"| Planned |- ac:local-id="9686f1ff23ab" | ac:local-id="f6c87539ed6a"| Draft standardized interview feedback template (Confluence / ATS) | ac:local-id="36e48c654e58"| MK | ac:local-id="07efaa0c9b0b"| 28 Mar | ac:local-id="75ac5bca4fcb"| in Progress |- ac:local-id="5f37cbd3629d" | ac:local-id="54acffeb27e0"| Design pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding plan | ac:local-id="46fb46119c32"| LF | ac:local-id="dd119c1f458c"| 1 Apr | ac:local-id="6abb58f7db11"| Not Started |- ac:local-id="9d6511dd48fa" | ac:local-id="ed92bdbbec91"| Create initial onboarding knowledge space in Confluence | ac:local-id="b6659a62cf10"| DW | ac:local-id="09a0f312f584"| 25 Mar | ac:local-id="9d75de32f3e5"| Planned |- ac:local-id="1d0c8cee6a05" | ac:local-id="43c46b18f57f"| Prepare communication plan for Employee Engagement Survey | ac:local-id="5308f2a862f3"| AS | ac:local-id="50acc1e692de"| 10 Apr | ac:local-id="3e2723fd8a4a"| In Progress |- ac:local-id="b0f89a6fe194" | ac:local-id="349af9354a7c"| Draft concept and agenda for first Quarterly Learning Day | ac:local-id="a96b63fd4c90"| DW | ac:local-id="51682a799aae"| 31 Mar | ac:local-id="f78ee64367db"| Not Started |- ac:local-id="379a4c665303" | ac:local-id="0aef6b74e314"| Review onboarding request process with IT to reduce tool access delays | ac:local-id="ab2e5dcca0fe"| LF | ac:local-id="f737ac88f437"| 15 Apr | ac:local-id="6bc7f64758a5"| Planned |- ac:local-id="b4c071dcea33" | ac:local-id="73a9ee9c0a87"| Research potential onboarding success metrics and reporting approach | ac:local-id="05ad1d9b1730"| DW | ac:local-id="c386305d9619"| 5 Apr | ac:local-id="4002feb23afb"| Not Started |} == Decisions Made == * HR will introduce '''weekly recruitment status updates''' for hiring managers to improve transparency and alignment. * Hiring managers are expected to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * HR will '''pilot a structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' will be created to provide key resources for new employees. * HR will move forward with planning the '''Employee Engagement Survey in April'''. * The team will explore introducing a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support internal knowledge sharing and employee development. == Open Questions == * Should manager training sessions become a '''mandatory program for newly promoted managers'''? * Which '''metrics should be used to measure onboarding success''' (e.g., satisfaction surveys, time to productivity)? * What is the best format for '''Quarterly Learning Day''' (internal workshops, guest speakers, hybrid format)? * How frequently should HR '''review and update the hybrid work policy'''? == Parking Lot (Topics for Later) == * Employer branding initiatives for the second half of the year * Internship and graduate program structure * HR reporting dashboard for leadership team * Internal mentorship program for new employees * Review of performance review process for next cycle == Attachments / Links == * Recruitment pipeline overview (March) * Draft onboarding checklist * Employee Engagement Survey – previous year results * HR initiatives roadmap (Q2) __NOTOC__

{| class="wikitable" layout="default" ac:local-id="d73dd568-4ccd-4f97-b47f-d3cb2c35de89" data-table-width="760" |- ac:local-id="95bc28a7b0f6" | ac:local-id="559e7ff62eb2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Meeting Title''' | ac:local-id="0b8264003b25"| HR Weekly Sync |- ac:local-id="e0fa4d088f8c" | ac:local-id="3845c3f9c1b0" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Date''' | ac:local-id="8fad56b65456"| 26 February 2024 |- ac:local-id="8ee98bc83bc8" | ac:local-id="015fee8bac9d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Time''' | ac:local-id="74c7117b3481"| 10:00–11:00 |- ac:local-id="59b64c968be4" | ac:local-id="f47f4ed481a2" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Location''' | ac:local-id="f6df72621c84"| Conference Room B / MS Teams |- ac:local-id="70d0c6e3fe6e" | ac:local-id="56bfd11c362d" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Facilitator''' | ac:local-id="3eb5cb522b94"| AS |- ac:local-id="89435e499f3b" | ac:local-id="0f4464fbfa90" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"| '''Note Taker''' | ac:local-id="7b352da1b447"| GH |} == Participants == === '''Attendees''' === * AS * MK * LF * DW * GH === '''Absent''' === * JB == Agenda == * Recruitment Pipeline Update * Onboarding Improvements * Upcoming HR Initiatives * Open Questions == Recruitment Pipeline Update == === '''Discussion''' === * MK shared an overview of the current recruitment pipeline. At the moment, there are '''12 open roles''' across the Engineering and Product departments. * The majority of open positions are in the Engineering team, including Backend, DevOps, and QA roles. Product currently has openings for '''1 Product Manager and 1 Product Designer'''. * Recruitment activity has increased compared to the previous month due to new project initiatives and team expansion plans. * MK reported that '''two candidates have progressed to the final interview stage for the Senior Backend Developer position'''. * Both candidates completed the technical interview successfully and are scheduled to meet the Engineering Director later this week. * The hiring manager indicated that a final decision is expected by '''early next week''', assuming reference checks are completed on time. * The team also discussed '''communication challenges between HR and hiring managers'''. In several cases, feedback from interviewers was delayed, which slowed down candidate progression in the recruitment pipeline. === '''Key Points''' === * 12 open roles currently active (Engineering and Product). * Two final-stage candidates for the '''Senior Backend Developer''' role. * Interview feedback from hiring managers is sometimes delayed. * Delays can negatively impact candidate experience and time-to-hire. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will send '''weekly recruitment status updates''' to hiring managers to improve transparency. * Hiring managers will be reminded to '''submit interview feedback within 48 hours''' after interviews. * MK will explore creating a '''standardized interview feedback form in Confluence or the ATS''' to streamline the process. }} == Onboarding Improvements == === '''Discussion''' === * LF shared feedback collected from '''recent hires during their first month'''. Overall onboarding satisfaction is positive, but several new employees mentioned that '''access to internal tools (e.g., project management software, documentation systems)''' was delayed during their first days. * The delays are mainly caused by missing information in the onboarding request form that HR sends to IT before a new employee starts. * LF proposed introducing a '''structured 30-60-90 day onboarding plan''' for all new employees. The goal is to provide clearer expectations for both managers and new hires regarding learning goals, responsibilities, and milestones. * DW added that a structured onboarding framework could also help managers who do not regularly hire new team members and may not have a defined onboarding process. * The team also discussed creating a '''central onboarding space in Confluence''' where new employees can access important information such as: ** company policies ** team introductions ** key tools and documentation ** learning resources === '''Key Points''' === * New hires reported '''delayed access to some internal tools'''. * Managers currently use '''different onboarding approaches''', leading to inconsistent experiences. * A standardized onboarding framework could improve '''clarity and consistency'''. * Confluence could serve as a '''central onboarding knowledge hub'''. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will design a '''pilot 30-60-90 day onboarding structure''' to test with upcoming hires. * A '''central onboarding page in Confluence''' will be created to collect essential information for new employees. * HR will review the '''current onboarding request process with IT''' to reduce access delays. }} == Upcoming HR Initiatives == === '''Discussion''' === * AS provided an overview of several '''planned HR initiatives for the upcoming quarter'''. * The first initiative is the '''annual Employee Engagement Survey''', which is planned for April. The goal of the survey is to gather feedback on employee satisfaction, work environment, and internal communication. * HR plans to analyze the results and share key insights with department leaders. * The second initiative is a '''Quarterly Learning Day''', where employees can participate in workshops, training sessions, and knowledge-sharing activities. DW suggested inviting internal speakers from different teams to present topics related to their expertise. * The team also discussed a '''review of the current hybrid work policy'''. Some managers have requested clearer guidelines regarding remote work expectations and office presence. === '''Key Points''' === * Employee Engagement Survey planned for April. * HR exploring a '''Quarterly Learning Day''' to support employee development. * Hybrid work policy may require updates or clarification. * Communication of HR initiatives should be improved to ensure employee awareness. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will begin preparing the '''Employee Engagement Survey communication plan'''. * DW will draft a '''concept for the first Quarterly Learning Day'''. * HR leadership will schedule a '''policy review meeting regarding hybrid work'''. }} == Open Questions == === '''Discussion''' === * * The team discussed whether HR should introduce '''regular training sessions for managers''' focused on topics such as giving feedback, conducting performance reviews, and supporting team development. * Several team members noted that '''newly promoted managers often request guidance''' on people management topics. Providing structured learning opportunities could help them develop the necessary skills. * Another topic raised was whether HR should begin '''tracking onboarding success metrics''', such as: ** new hire satisfaction ** time to productivity ** early employee turnover * These metrics could help HR better evaluate the effectiveness of onboarding and identify potential improvements. === '''Key Points''' === * Potential need for '''manager training sessions'''. * Interest in measuring '''onboarding effectiveness through defined metrics'''. * HR should explore which metrics are most relevant and how to collect them. === '''Outcome / Decision''' === {{Note |body = * HR will explore options for '''quarterly manager training sessions'''. * DW will research possible '''onboarding success metrics and reporting methods'''. * Topic will be revisited in the next HR strategy meeting }} == Action Items == {| class="wikitable" layout="default"
